andvari·Frontend & UI Design Audit

2026-07-12 · dev-ledger/andvari @ 46e1e4e · 5 surfaces · 8 lenses

The treasury, audited.

One coherent brand, five hand-built clients, and 131 verified places where the craft is uneven. A design/UX audit of andvari across web, extension, Android, desktop, and its server-side email — surfaced by a multi-agent sweep and adversarially verified.

131verified findings · run 1126 confirmed · 5 plausible · +69 new in the blind replication
0criticalno data-loss / security-breaking UI defects
9high severitya11y, trust-UX & the design-system split
severity distribution
High 9Medium 47Low 56Nit 19

Verdict

andvari has a strong, coherent brand concept and genuinely good security primitives — zero-knowledge design, clipboard auto-clear, a shown-once recovery ceremony — but the execution is applied unevenly across its five surfaces. Web is the reference implementation (it alone has an AA-tuned --link token, tofu-proof SVG brand geometry, a two-tone wordmark, and a contrast unit test); the extension, Android and desktop clients are hand-synced copies of the design system that have already drifted, and the server-side HTML/email shell was never in the accessibility pass at all. The single biggest through-line is that the design language lives in five disjoint hand-maintained files with no shared seam and a contrast test that guards only two tokens — so light-theme WCAG AA failures on security-critical text (worst: Android's primary error banner at 2.56:1, roughly half the floor), invisible focus indicators, dead tokens, and baseline-Material lavender bleed have all shipped uncaught. The brand's own tofu-proofing decision (drawing the rune as SVG) was made only on web, so the primary mark risks rendering as empty boxes at every trust-critical screen on native and the injected overlay. Trust-UX is the other soft spot: the core copy action gives no feedback and the auto-clear window is never communicated, onboarding over-states a printed recovery sheet the default emailed (waived) flow never produces, and the invite email — every member's first contact — arrives as an unbranded no-reply plaintext message indistinguishable from a phishing lure. Native lags the most and is the hardest to visually iterate, so defects that look fine in dark mode hide in light mode and in un-overridden Material container roles. Encouragingly, most of this is cheap: the majority of findings are pure color-value or copy changes, and the highest-leverage structural move is consolidating the palette into one generated token source with a broadened contrast test so these defect classes cannot regress.

Cross-cutting themes10

High

Fragmented design system — five hand-synced token copies with no shared seam

The palette is re-declared verbatim across web CSS, extension popup.css, the content-ui overlay, Android Theme.kt and desktop Main.kt with nothing mechanically coupling them; sync is comment-only and per-file contrast tests never assert cross-file equality. This is the root cause of nearly every divergence below — drift has already shipped as AA failures, dead tokens, and light-scheme gaps.

High

Light-theme WCAG AA contrast failures on gold/danger/faint ink and focus indicators

Gold-as-text (~3.7-4.05:1), danger text on tinted strips (~4.39:1), Android's error banner on errorContainer (2.56:1), and light-mode focus rings (~2:1) all fall below AA/3:1 across web, extension, Android and desktop — concentrated on the exact wrong-password/lock/error text a security tool most needs legible. The contrast tests give a false green because they only check two tokens.

High

Native Material theming incomplete — baseline purple/pink bleeds into the gold treasury

Android and desktop override only the legacy base color roles, leaving M3 container/tertiary/errorContainer roles at Material baseline, so Cards, the Add FAB, dialogs and warnings render in stock lavender/mauve and the light scheme drops surfaceVariant/onSurfaceVariant. The treasury identity is largely absent on-device — the generic Material look the brand is trying to avoid.

High

Clipboard auto-clear and copy actions give no feedback; sensitive seeds never clear

Copy — the core daily action — produces no in-app confirmation on native/desktop and the auto-wipe window is never communicated on any surface, so a late paste silently fails and a real safety feature is undiscoverable. Worse, Settings' TOTP seed/otpauth copies never auto-clear at all.

High

Onboarding stakes under-communicated — recovery-sheet copy is wrong for the default flow

The first enroll gate (and the invite email) tell every invitee they need a printed recovery sheet, but the default emailed/waived flow generates the member's own phrase on-device and produces no sheet — stalling the most important ceremony. 'Recovery' is overloaded four ways and forgotten-password paths dead-end.

High

Unbranded plaintext invite email is a phishing-indistinguishable first contact

The highest-stakes first message arrives as unstyled plaintext from a raw no-reply mailbox with no inviter name, no wordmark, no anti-phishing inoculation line, and an opaque 223-char link — a recipient has no legitimacy signal to tell it from a credential-harvest lure.

Medium

Brand mark ships as raw runic codepoints (tofu risk) on every surface but web

Web draws ᛅ/ᛝ as SVG geometry precisely because the codepoints tofu where no Runic font is installed and CSP bars web fonts; the extension, Android (Noto Serif has no Runic block) and desktop all print the raw codepoint as text, re-introducing the exact failure at loading, unlock, empty-state and the anti-spoof overlay.

Medium

Screen-reader/keyboard a11y gaps in dynamic states

Desktop has zero live regions; Android autofill errors are silent; copy buttons announce a bare 'Copy'; search fields and the recovery-confirm input lack programmatic labels; busy states aren't announced and disabling drops focus; headings/landmarks/skip links are missing. Static contrast was largely fixed but the async and control-naming layer wasn't.

Low

Member-facing copy leaks ops/dev jargon

'break-glass', 'the public address', 'escrow', 'TOTP secret (otpauth:// URI or base32)', 'Enroll', 'Invite token', and cvv/exp/num abbreviations land on the most anxiety-prone security screens of a warm-household product, clashing with the established 'unsealing your hoard' voice.

Low

Absent first-impression, platform-fit and progress polish

No favicon/theme-color/apple-touch-icon/PWA manifest/OG meta/noscript on the web shell; no 'Unsealing…' progress affordance during the multi-second KDF; desktop MSI/Deb ship the stock Compose icon; and within-surface drift (7-15 font sizes, seven corner radii, ragged coin monograms, single-color wordmark on native) undercuts the crafted-treasury feel.

The headline

One design language — the “treasury/gold” system — is re-declared verbatim in five disjoint files with no shared seam. Sync is comment-only; the per-file contrast tests never assert cross-file equality. Drift has already shipped as WCAG failures, dead tokens, and light-scheme gaps.

#d0a94agoldaccent
#e8c66agold-brightsheen
#14120ebgground
#1e1b15surfaceraised
#ede4d0inktext
#a79c85ink-dimmuted
#8d8370ink-faintAA-tuned
#cf6b5adangererror
web/src/ui/styles.cssorigin — CSS custom props
extension/popup.csshand-copy #2
extension/src/content-ui.tshand-copy #3 · ink-faint stuck pre-AA (#6f6752), no contrast test
app-android/…/Theme.kthand-copy #4 · Material roles un-overridden
app-desktop/…/Main.kthand-copy #5
9 cross-platform findings
HighFive hand-synced copies of the design system with no shared seam — the SSOT is a comment, and drift has already shipped

The design language (22 color tokens x2 themes, 3 font stacks, radius, shadow, plus component idioms) is physically duplicated across 5 disjoint files in 3 languages (CSS, TS template-string, Kotlin). Nothing mechanically couples them. This is the root cause of every other divergence below: the overlay AA lag, the native light-mode gaps, the onPrimary mismatch, the gold-link AA fail. The existing 'lockstep by convention' has already failed at least three times (findings 2, 3, 4). For a password manager whose whole brand promise is a single trustworthy 'treasury' surface across web/extension/mobile/desktop, uncontrolled drift directly erodes the anti-spoof / recognizability value the team explicitly designed for (content-ui.ts:13 calls the treasury surface an anti-spoof signal).

Evidence Same palette re-declared verbatim in web/src/ui/styles.css:5-65, extension/popup.css:6-56, extension/src/content-ui.ts:22-59 (--anv-*), app-android/.../app/Theme.kt:16-49, app-desktop/.../desktop/Main.kt:31-46. Sync is enforced only by hand-written comments ('ported verbatim from web/src/ui/styles.css' popup.css:1; 'Keep in lockstep with the extension's popup.css twin' styles.css:18). No import, no codegen, no cross-file test — web/src/ui/contrast.test.ts:16 reads only styles.css, extension/src/contrast.test.ts:31 reads only popup.css, and BOTH only assert each file independently clears AA; neither asserts the copies are EQUAL. So any two copies can silently diverge as long as each stays above AA (or isn't covered at all).

Fix Ship BOTH, in order. (1) INTERIM, hours of work: one node lockstep test that parses all five files (hex regexes + the Kotlin Color(0xFF..) form), normalizes to a canonical {dark,light} map, and asserts every shared token matches — encoding the two known legitimate remaps (overlay --anv-bg == web --bg-raised, --anv-bg-deep == web --bg; native = documented subset). Fail CI on any mismatch. Extend its ratio helper to also AA-check the overlay tokens (closes the blind spot that let finding 2 through). This immediately makes drift a red build. (2) REAL FIX, ~0.5-1 day: a canonical design/tokens.json (semantic key -> {dark,light} + type + radius) and design/build-tokens.mjs that GENERATES web tokens.css (imported by styles.css), a popup tokens block, extension/src/tokens.generated.ts (the --anv-* string, remap applied in the generator), and AndvariColors.kt (shared Compose Colors + darkColorScheme/lightColorScheme for android+desktop). CI runs `node build-tokens.mjs && git diff --exit-code`. Contrast test then runs once over tokens.json for ALL surfaces. Style Dictionary is an off-the-shelf alternative but a ~120-line script keeps the self-contained/no-dep posture.

MediumOverlay --anv-ink-faint is stuck at the pre-AA value on both themes, and the overlay is covered by NO contrast test

This is the concrete proof that the hand-sync model has already broken. The a11yweb-03/'A1 7a' contrast fix was applied to web and popup but the third web-family copy was missed, and because the overlay has zero contrast coverage the miss was never caught. Today it's latent (dead token), but the moment anyone styles an overlay muted element with var(--anv-ink-faint) — the obvious choice for a timestamp or secondary line — the extension ships sub-AA text on arbitrary sites, the highest-visibility surface.

Evidence extension/src/content-ui.ts:30 `--anv-ink-faint: #6f6752;` (dark) and :51 `--anv-ink-faint: #9a8f74;` (light) are the OLD values web+popup abandoned for WCAG AA: styles.css:19/:48 and popup.css:20/:44 both carry #8d8370 (dark, ~5.0:1) / #786c50 (light, ~4.5:1). #6f6752 on the overlay base #1e1b15 is ~3.3:1 and #9a8f74 on #fbf8f1 is ~2.8:1 — both fail AA. Neither contrast test reads content-ui.ts, so this is invisible to CI. (Mitigating: grep shows --anv-ink-faint is currently defined-but-unused in the overlay CSS, so no live text renders sub-AA today — but it is a loaded gun.)

Fix Immediately bump content-ui.ts:30 to #8d8370 and :51 to #786c50 to match web/popup. Then fold the overlay into the lockstep + AA test from finding 1 so it can never drift or regress silently again. If the token is genuinely unused, still fix it (cheap) rather than leave a sub-AA landmine.

MediumPopup and overlay color interactive text with raw --gold and never adopted web's AA-tuned --link token → link text fails WCAG AA in light mode, untested

The same interactive-text-in-gold pattern that web deliberately darkened for AA is shipped un-darkened on popup and overlay. Users on the light theme get link/action text that fails AA — and there is no test guarding it, so it will stay broken and could regress further.

Evidence Web introduced a dedicated --link (styles.css:26 dark #d0a94a == --gold; :55 light #7d5e14, distinct from --gold #9a7420) specifically so links clear AA in light without flattening the button gradient — and pins it in web/src/ui/contrast.test.ts:71-72. Popup never adopted it: popup.css .link uses var(--gold) (footer 'Open web vault' etc.). Overlay never adopted it: content-ui.ts:109 .row.action and :255 .site-link use var(--anv-gold). In LIGHT mode gold = #9a7420: computed #9a7420 on popup --bg #f4efe4 ≈ 3.74:1 and on overlay base #fbf8f1 ≈ 3.9:1 — both below AA 4.5:1. Popup's contrast test only covers --ink-faint/--ink-dim (contrast.test.ts:57-64); the overlay has no test at all. So the exact defect web fixed with --link is live and unmonitored on two surfaces.

Fix Add --link / --anv-link to popup.css and content-ui.ts (dark = gold, light = #7d5e14) and point .link / .row.action / .site-link at it, mirroring web. Cover link tokens for all three web-family surfaces in the finding-1 contrast test. This is a real a11y fix, not only a consistency nicety.

MediumNative light color scheme silently drops surfaceVariant + onSurfaceVariant → tagline, empty-state and timestamps render in Material default grey (off-brand) in light mode

A dark/light asymmetry in the native palette means the light theme leaks Material's stock purple-grey into the treasury aesthetic on exactly the branded copy (tagline, empty hoard). Dark mode looks correct so this is easy to miss in review — and it is the second independent instance of the hand-sync model failing.

Evidence Android Theme.kt dark sets surfaceVariant=#262119 (:33) and onSurfaceVariant=#A79C85 (=--ink-dim), but the light block (:39-49) omits BOTH; identical asymmetry in desktop Main.kt (dark :39 has them, light :43-45 does not). Meanwhile onSurfaceVariant is the color used for real brand text: the tagline 'the keeper of the hoard' (MainActivity.kt:353, Ui.kt:140) and empty-state subtitle 'Your hoard is empty.' (MainActivity.kt:849, Ui.kt:533) both use colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant. In light mode those fall back to Material3's default onSurfaceVariant (~#49454F, a purple-grey) instead of andvari's --ink-dim #6b6047, and surfaceVariant-tinted containers get Material's ~#E7E0EC instead of the warm input tone.

Fix Add surfaceVariant (#FFFFFF or #FBF8F1 to taste) and onSurfaceVariant (#6B6047 = --ink-dim) to both light schemes (Theme.kt:39-49, Main.kt:42-46). Once tokens.json/AndvariColors.kt (finding 1) generates the Kotlin schemes, dark/light symmetry is guaranteed by construction.

MediumBrand sigil rendering strategy diverges — web draws tofu-proof SVG geometry; popup, overlay and native all render the raw runic codepoints ᛅ/ᛝ (the exact tofu risk web engineered away)

The one place a strong tofu-proofing decision was made (web SVG) is also the only place it was applied. The other four surfaces re-introduce the failure mode the SVG was built to prevent, so the primary brand mark can silently disappear into a box on the surfaces most exposed to unknown font environments — weakening both brand recognition and the documented anti-spoof rationale.

Evidence web/src/ui/Sigil.tsx draws BrandSigil ᛅ and EmptySigil ᛝ as inline SVG paths, with a comment stating the reason: 'The runic codepoints render as tofu wherever no installed font covers the Runic block, and the CSP posture bars shipping one.' Every other surface ignores that and prints the codepoint as text: popup.html:10,36 + popup.ts:228 (`sigil.textContent = 'ᛅ'`), content-ui.ts:275 & :507 (`span('sigil','ᛅ')`), and native MainActivity.kt:351 / Ui.kt:138 (`Text('ᛅ', ...)`) plus empty-state ᛝ at MainActivity.kt:849 / Ui.kt:533. The Runic block is absent from most default system fonts (e.g. Android's Noto Serif, most Windows serifs), so the mark renders as a tofu box precisely on the extension overlay (arbitrary sites) and native — where content-ui.ts:13 relies on the 'distinctive treasury surface' as an ANTI-SPOOF attribution signal.

Fix Port the Sigil geometry to every surface: emit the two SVG paths from popup.ts/content-ui.ts (both already build DOM — swap textContent 'ᛅ' for an inline <svg>), and on native draw the same paths with Compose Canvas/Icon (or bundle a tiny app-local vector) instead of Text('ᛅ'). Keep the path data in one place (a shared constant, or generated alongside tokens.json). Fixes a latent rendering bug and unifies the mark.

LowPrimary-button corner radius is three different values across surfaces despite a shared 10px radius token

The 10px 'house radius' is honored inconsistently: the overlay bypasses its own token with magic numbers, and native never sees the token because there is no Compose Shapes override. Minor visually but it's drift the token was meant to prevent.

Evidence web button.primary border-radius: var(--radius)=10px (styles.css:160). popup button.primary border-radius: var(--radius)=10px (popup.css:131). Overlay button.primary HARDCODES border-radius: 8px (content-ui.ts:150) and .ghost 8px (:162) even though --anv-radius: 10px is defined (:39) and used elsewhere (:74,:135); its search-input is 7px (:121). Native has no shapes override at all, so Buttons use Material3's default stadium/large radius. Net: the 'same' primary button is 10px on web/popup, 8px in the overlay, and fully-rounded on android/desktop.

Fix Change overlay button/ghost/search radii to var(--anv-radius) (or a deliberate --anv-radius-sm token if 8px is intended). On native, add a Shapes override to MaterialTheme (small=10.dp etc.) so buttons/cards match the 10px language. Route all of it through tokens.json.

LowNative onPrimary in light mode is pure white; web's equivalent --btn-ink is warm off-white #fdf9f0

A small warm-vs-pure-white mismatch on primary-button text in light mode. Barely perceptible in isolation but it is one more uncontrolled divergence and trivially avoidable.

Evidence Android Theme.kt:41 and desktop Main.kt:43 both set light onPrimary = Color.White (#FFFFFF). Web/popup light --btn-ink = #fdf9f0 (styles.css:60, popup.css:50) — a warm paper white, not pure white. Dark mode matches exactly (#1A1509 == --btn-ink dark), so only light diverges. (Android and desktop remain byte-identical to each other.)

Fix Set native light onPrimary = Color(0xFFFDF9F0) to match --btn-ink. Auto-resolved once AndvariColors.kt is generated from tokens.json.

LowTypography diverges: native uses generic Serif/Monospace families and overrides only 2 type roles; the overlay abridges the curated serif/mono stacks

The intent (serif for display, mono for secrets) is preserved everywhere, but the actual typefaces differ by platform and the overlay quietly ships a shorter fallback chain. Low impact given all fonts must be system fonts anyway, but it means the display serif and secret mono look materially different between web and native.

Evidence Web/popup define curated stacks: serif = 'Iowan Old Style', 'Palatino Linotype', Palatino, 'Book Antiqua', Georgia, serif and mono = 'SF Mono','JetBrains Mono','Fira Code',ui-monospace,'Cascadia Code',monospace (styles.css:6-8). Overlay ABRIDGES them: content-ui.ts:36 drops 'Palatino Linotype'/'Book Antiqua'; :38 drops 'Fira Code'/'Cascadia Code'. Native uses generic FontFamily.Serif (Theme.kt:51 — resolves to Noto Serif on Android, not Iowan/Palatino) and per-widget FontFamily.Monospace (generic, e.g. MainActivity.kt:2125), never the curated mono stack; and AndvariType overrides only headlineMedium(26sp) + titleLarge(22sp) (Theme.kt:52-55, Main.kt:47-50), leaving all body/label roles as Material defaults.

Fix Restore the full serif/mono stacks in the overlay (copy verbatim from styles.css, or generate from tokens.json). On native, accept the generic-family constraint but document it as intentional, and consider defining a mono TextStyle token so 'secrets in mono' is a theme decision, not scattered per-widget FontFamily.Monospace. Keep the two type roles in tokens.json for the record.

NitOverlay token names invert the surface hierarchy (--anv-bg = the RAISED tone), a copy-by-name trap; contrast tests never assert cross-file equality

A latent sync hazard: the one place the overlay legitimately remaps semantics is undocumented at the token site and unguarded by tests, so a well-meaning 'keep in sync' edit could invert the overlay's background/foreground.

Evidence content-ui.ts:23 `--anv-bg: #1e1b15;` (which is web's --bg-RAISED) and :24 `--anv-bg-deep: #14120e;` (web's --bg). So --anv-bg != --bg by name. Intentional (the overlay floats on pages, so its base is the card tone) but it means a maintainer syncing values by matching token NAMES between overlay and web would swap the two surfaces. Compounding it, web/src/ui/contrast.test.ts and extension/src/contrast.test.ts each validate only their own file's AA and never compare copies, so no test would catch such a swap.

Fix Add a comment at content-ui.ts:23-24 stating the deliberate remap (--anv-bg = web --bg-raised), and encode that remap explicitly in the finding-1 lockstep test so the relationship is machine-checked rather than tribal knowledge.

What to fix first15

Ranked by ROI (severity × inverse-effort). Effort: S hours · M a day or two · L multi-day.

#FixSurfacesSevEffWhy
1 Repaint the AA-failing color tokens (error-on-errorContainer, light gold text, danger tint strips) and extend the contrast tests to cover them androidextensionwebdesktop HighS Shipped WCAG failures on security-critical text — worst is Android's error banner at 2.56:1 (half the floor) — are trivial hex swaps the current two-token test cannot catch.
2 Add a dedicated ≥3:1 focus-indicator token and a real :focus-visible rule for inputs, rows and buttons in both themes webextensiondesktop HighS Keyboard and low-vision users cannot see which field/row is focused anywhere in light mode (~2:1); one --focus token fixes every form and table.
3 Surface clipboard auto-clear ('copied — clears in 30s') + a copy confirmation, and route Settings TOTP-seed/otpauth copies through the auto-clearing path webextensionandroiddesktop HighS The core daily action gives no feedback and the silent auto-wipe reads as a failed paste, while the most sensitive seeds never clear at all.
4 Rebuild the invite email: inviter display name + branded From, anti-phishing inoculation line, posture-aware recovery-sheet copy, and concrete 'open on your home network' guidance server-html HighM Every member's first contact is an unbranded no-reply plaintext lure that also tells the default waived flow to wait for a printed sheet that never exists.
5 Make onboarding recovery copy posture-aware across clients, disambiguate the four 'recovery' terms, and add a locked-screen recover/enroll signpost webandroiddesktopextension HighS The first gate over-states a printed sheet the emailed (waived) default never produces, stranding invitees, and forgotten-password paths dead-end with no pointer.
6 Stand up one generated design-token source (tokens.json → CSS/TS/Kotlin) with a cross-file lockstep + full-token contrast test cross-platform HighM Five hand-synced palette copies have already drifted into AA fails, dead tokens and light-scheme gaps with nothing to catch it — this closes the whole class by construction.
7 Complete the native Material color schemes — container/tertiary/errorContainer roles, gold FAB, light surfaceVariant/onSurfaceVariant androiddesktop HighM Baseline Material lavender/pink currently paints Cards, the Add FAB and warnings, so the treasury identity is largely absent on the surface most people use daily.
8 Port the tofu-proof SVG BrandSigil/EmptySigil geometry to extension, Android and desktop (ᛝ for empty state, monochrome die glyph) extensionandroiddesktop HighM The primary brand mark risks rendering as empty tofu boxes at every trust-critical screen on the font-hostile surfaces, contradicting the team's own reason for shipping it as SVG.
9 Add live regions + programmatic labels + headings/landmarks for dynamic states (desktop has none) desktopandroidwebextension HighM Screen-reader users get no spoken feedback on wrong-password/error/lock and cannot distinguish identical 'Copy' controls; desktop announces nothing async at all.
10 Add a progress/'Unsealing…' affordance + aria-busy during the multi-second Argon2id derivation and keep the button focusable webextensionandroiddesktop MediumS The primary CTA looks frozen for several seconds with no motion or reassuring voice and drops keyboard focus when it disables.
11 Add favicon + theme-color + apple-touch-icon + a branded noscript from the rune (optional PWA manifest/OG) webserver-html LowS Pinned tabs show the generic globe and JS-off yields an empty page — a cheap first-impression and trust win for a security tool, using assets that already exist.
12 De-jargon member-facing copy (break-glass/public address, escrow, 'TOTP secret (otpauth…)', Enroll/Invite token, cvv/exp abbreviations) webextensionandroid MediumS Internal ops/dev vocabulary lands on the most anxiety-prone security screens in a product whose voice is warm and household-metaphored.
13 Close dead-ends and guard irreversible actions: desktop UpgradeRequired/recovery/session escape hatches, Generate 'Replace?' confirm, extension popup overflow (sticky Lock) + TOTP-chip latch desktopextension MediumM Users get stranded on screens they can't leave and one stray Generate click silently overwrites a live credential.
14 Android/desktop parity: manual sync feedback + pull-to-refresh, honest 'add members on the web' signpost, friendly offline sign-in errors, read-only breach/HIBP health card androiddesktop MediumL Mobile-primary members silently lack core trust features (health, sharing) and see raw exception strings on their first connection.
15 Visual-cohesion sweep: collapse type scales and corner radii to tokens, shared coin-monogram component, two-tone gold wordmark, reduced-motion support, desktop app/launcher icons webextensionandroiddesktop LowM Accumulated within-surface drift (7-15 font sizes, seven radii, ragged monograms, single-color wordmark) undercuts the crafted-treasury feel.

Blind replication — run 2196 verified

On the owner's request the entire audit was re-run blind: a clean checkout of the same commit (pre-fixes), 25 fresh finder agents with a different lens partition and no access to run 1's results, an independent consistency stage, and the same adversarial verification (233 raw → 196 confirmed, 15 rejected). Six matcher agents then reconciled the two runs finding-by-finding.

81run-1 findings rediscoveredindependent confirmation, incl. a 9/9 perfect match on the cross-platform design-system findings
69substantive NEW defectsreal distinct issues run 1 missed — detailed below (+33 variant angles, +15 minor)
49not rediscoveredrun-1 findings run 2 didn't hit — still valid (verified in run 1); coverage variance cuts both ways
~200unique defects combinedthe union of both runs — neither pass alone was exhaustive

Run 2 independently converged on every structural theme of run 1 — the five-copy design system, light-theme AA failures, half-themed Compose clients, the unbranded invite email — and surfaced new defect classes run 1's lens split under-weighted: one-tap data loss (unguarded editor discards, unconfirmed sign-out wiping unsynced edits, one-click history Restore), leaky recovery ceremonies (the capture gate defeated by its own Copy button; desktop's "un-skippable" gate skippable via auto-lock), silent core-loop feedback (fills that claim success without filling, feedback-free email dispatch), and platform-fit basics (Android SDK-35 insets, missing KeyboardType.Password on secret fields, IME-blocked Unlock).

Combined action plan v2 — 27 items (merged backlog from both runs)
#FixSurfacesSevEffWhy
1 Accessible-gold + focus-token sweep for the light theme webextensionandroiddesktopcross-platform HighS Every light-mode link, button label, tab, fill pill, and focus ring sits at 2.1–4.3:1; swapping token values to the ~#7d5e14 family (already proven by web's --link fix) plus per-theme focus tokens clears ~10 findings across all four clients in one pass.
2 Invite email overhaul through one shared InviteEmail.compose() server-html HighS The first-contact email is factually wrong for 100% of UI-emailed invites (phantom recovery sheet), anonymous, jargon-laden, vague on its 60-minute fuse, and hand-duplicated across two transports — one shared compose(escrowPolicy, expiresAt, inviterName) fixes six findings at the single point future edits land.
3 Add the missing one-dialog confirms: editor cancel (Android), sign-out (Android + desktop), version-history Restore (web + desktop) androiddesktopweb HighS These are the only unguarded destructive taps in an otherwise confirm-disciplined product — one dialog each in the existing idiom stops one-tap loss of typed drafts, the offline cache with unsynced edits, and live passwords.
4 Secret-field input hygiene: KeyboardType.Password everywhere (Android) + autocomplete suppression in the web editor androidweb HighS Zero Android fields declare Password, so IMEs can echo and cloud-learn master passwords; the web editor's bare password field invites Chrome's own password manager inside the vault — both fix in one shared composable / two attributes.
5 Android edge-to-edge insets + scrollable Unlock/overlay columns android HighS Targeting SDK 35 with zero insets handling draws the security banner stack under the status bar on Android 15+, and non-scrolling centered columns make the Unlock button unreachable with the keyboard open on fold/landscape — the app's most-used screen.
6 'Forgot your master password?' affordance on every locked surface androiddesktopextension HighS Locked-out members currently see only a destructive sign-out or an infinite wrong-password loop while onboarding explicitly promised self-recovery; a static link to the web ceremony ends the dead-end on all three clients.
7 Harden the recovery capture gates against copy→paste webandroiddesktop HighS The shown-once ceremony's fastest path records the phrase as 'durably saved' while it exists only on a clipboard the app itself wipes 30s later — block/deter paste, fix the placeholder that literally invites it, and chunk the 43-char phrase for transcription.
8 Web small-screen CSS batch: admin invite row, nav overflow fade, auth-shell gutter, mobile toolbar, scoped label caps web HighS Five verified phone-breaking defects — a crushed email field on the admin's primary action, an invisible Admin tab, an edge-to-edge sign-in card, a ragged toolbar, and multi-line ALL-CAPS consent sentences — are each a few lines of CSS.
9 Desktop window sanity: max content width, minimum window size, clickable 426/banner URL desktop HighS Maximized windows stretch password fields into 200+ character lines, small windows silently clip Lock and Sync off the toolbar, and the fully blocking update screen makes users hand-transcribe a URL painted in link color — three near-one-line fixes.
10 Stateful copy + disclosed clipboard auto-clear via one shared helper per platform webextensionandroiddesktop HighM The product's most-repeated action silently succeeds and then silently un-does itself; 'Copied — clears in 30s', failure handling, visible TOTP copy buttons, and secret-copy parity (invite tokens, 2FA seeds get EXTRA_IS_SENSITIVE/auto-clear) fold ~12 findings into one pattern.
11 Complete both Compose color schemes (+values-night themes.xml) androiddesktop HighM Error text at 2.56:1, Material-pink recovery ceremonies, an invisible security Switch, and lavender dialogs all trace to ~18 undeclared M3 roles — one theme-file completion per client (plus warm tertiary/errorContainer ramps) fixes ~10 findings at once.
12 Cross-client token parity test + shared contrast spec table (styles.css, popup.css, exported UI_CSS, Kotlin golden test) cross-platform HighM Five hand-synced palette declarations have already drifted three ways — once inside a single 'cross-client' commit, once into a live AA violation; machine-checked parity plus a role×surface contrast matrix is what makes ranks 1 and 11 permanent.
13 Enrollment-flow repairs: parse the emailed enroll link on desktop; fix posture-error copy (web) and sheet-provenance copy (android) desktopwebandroid HighM First-run currently demands a bare 'invite token' the email never contained, routes fixable posture mismatches to an admin round-trip instead of the on-form toggle, and tells invitees the inviter's printed sheet is their own — all on the make-or-break first session.
14 Extension fill-outcome feedback end to end extension HighM The flagship action fails silently (dropdown picks, no-form pages, SW restarts) or affirmatively fakes success (popup closes on message delivery, not fill); wire the existing toast, propagate a filled/no-form/refused outcome, and map raw 'locked'/'unknown item' strings through the error canon.
15 Desktop recovery-gate invariant: re-route on unconfirmed server flag + pre-lock protection for RecoverySetup and editor drafts desktop HighM The 'un-skippable' silent-total-loss guard is skippable today by the app's own idle lock (and quit/crash), and becomes a genuine unnoticed total-loss path the moment the waived posture ships — this must land first.
16 Web editor dirty guard + auto-lock pre-warning web HighM One accidental back-swipe, nav tap, or 15-minute idle lock silently destroys a typed note or a freshly generated password already set on a website — the single unguarded data-loss path left on the flagship client.
17 Decouple desktop background sync from the global busy flag + visible sync/attachment progress + HTTP timeouts desktop HighM Every window re-focus and 5-minute poll hijacks the user's own Save button into an anonymous spinner — unbounded when the server black-holes — while manual sync gives zero feedback and stacks concurrent runs.
18 Keyboard/AT operability for the in-page dropdown + announced save banners extension HighM The autofill dropdown is mousedown-only divs with no roles, focus, or announcements, and the only in-page save path self-dismisses silently after 30s — listbox semantics plus the already-built live regions fix the flagship interaction for keyboard and screen-reader users.
19 LazyColumn vault/trash lists + derived, debounced filters androiddesktop HighM Both Compose clients compose every vault item eagerly and re-filter synchronously per keystroke — the exact main-thread freeze class their own import dialogs cap at 100 rows — right after the promoted CSV import fills the vault with hundreds of items.
20 Surface the buried value: autofill onboarding offer + vault-context chips (Android), Sharing web signposts (Android + desktop), visible Health label (web) androiddesktopweb HighM The core daily feature is never offered at onboarding, personal vs household items are indistinguishable everywhere (the product-class privacy hazard), and screens named 'Sharing' cannot share with no pointer to the web surface that can.
21 Invite lifecycle visibility: emailStatus in InviteResponse + pending-invite list/revoke in Admin server-htmlweb HighM Sends, rate-limit skips, and transport failures (e.g. an expired Graph secret) are indistinguishable today — 'she never got it' is undebuggable — and a photographed QR is an unrevokable 72-hour bearer credential the code itself names as uncontained.
22 Web/server platform hygiene batch: favicon+manifest+theme-color, boot placeholder+noscript, asset-miss 404, cache/compression headers, robots + security headers, bootstrap-invite self-heal webserver-html MediumS Roughly 50 lines across index.html and App.kt remove the anonymous tab icon, the permanent-blank-page failure mode the SPA fallback masks, the full bundle re-download every visit, pentest-bait missing headers, and the 30-day bricked bootstrap edge case.
23 Household voice + error-string sweep with a shared glossary webextensionandroiddesktop MediumM Wire noise ('bad request', 'authentication failed', raw IOExceptions, epoch timestamps) and enterprise jargon ('org', 'break-glass', 'backup-cli', three meanings of 'trash', five names for TOTP) land at exactly the moments non-technical family members are most fragile; Android just needs web's existing strings mapped.
24 Motion/progress layer with prefers-reduced-motion (+ unblock desktop startup HTTP) webandroiddesktop MediumM Multi-second Argon2id unlocks, syncs, and downloads show literally nothing moving on any client — indistinguishable from a hang for this audience — and the desktop even freezes its UI thread up to ~8s on a synchronous update check (a one-line withContext fix that rides along).
25 Brand-mark robustness: port web's SVG sigils to extension + Compose, fix ᛅ→ᛝ empty states, align serif/type roles extensionandroiddesktop MediumM The trust-signaling rune renders as tofu on stock macOS/iOS/Linux exactly where anti-spoofing and first-launch impressions live, the two-rune vocabulary is muddled, and dialog/ceremony headings get the brand serif by coin-flip — web already solved the hard part with SVG geometry.
26 User theme override (Auto/Light/Dark) across surfaces webandroiddesktopserver-html LowM A preference gap rather than a defect, but Linux desktop users are hard-stuck in the weaker light theme; restructure tokens for a data-theme hook and sequence it after rank 1 so choosing light no longer means choosing sub-AA.
27 Platform-fit roadmap: extension quick-unlock tier, desktop menu bar + Ctrl+L, CMP screen-reader verification/documentation extensiondesktop MediumL The remaining structural friction — a 6-second full online re-unlock every 15 idle minutes, no panic-lock shortcut, and plausibly zero Linux screen-reader support — needs design and framework work, not patches; scope it deliberately rather than letting it block the S/M wins above.

The 69 substantive new findings

Web 17

HighUnsaved editor content is discarded with zero guard by one accidental Back press, nav click, or cancel tapMStates, feedback, motion & interaction quality

The editor can hold a long secure note, a freshly generated password, and staged attachments; a single accidental back-swipe or nav tap silently destroys it. The app is otherwise full of inline confirms, making this the one unguarded data-loss path and the likeliest one for the stated audience to hit.

Evidence web/src/ui/Vault.tsx:171-182 (closeTop + useBackGuard), :344 (nav), :1513, :1635 (cancel paths), :1377-1381 (doc/pending live only in local state); grep dirty/beforeunload over web/src/ui = 0 guards.

Fix Track doc/pending vs initial; when dirty, route closeTop/cancel/nav through the house inline-confirm ('Discard this unsaved item?'). Lock may legitimately skip it.

HighRecovery-phrase capture gate is defeated by its own Copy button: copy → paste → confirm, then the auto-wipe destroys the only copy (merged with the trust-cluster duplicate)SFlows, information architecture & content

The shown-once ceremony's fastest path is copy→paste→confirm: the app records the phrase as durably saved while it exists only on a clipboard the app itself wipes ~30s later. For a waived-escrow member this is exactly the silent total loss the gate was built to prevent — and the placeholder invites it.

Evidence web/src/ui/Welcome.tsx:712-722 (copy + scheduled wipe), :739 (Copy phrase), :742-752 (confirm input + placeholder), :755 (gate), :823-830 (confirm → confirmRegisteredRecovery + onReady). Same RecoveryReveal serves both enroll and the sign-in/unlock capture gate.

Fix At minimum change the placeholder ('type it from where you saved it'); preferably block paste on the confirm input or interpose an honest 'Where did you save it?' step when pasted text matches the clipboard copy.

HighAdmin 'Invite a user' packs 6 controls into one no-wrap row — the email input is crushed to a few characters and labels collideSTypography, spacing, layout & responsive

The admin's primary action lays out six controls on a single non-wrapping row inside the 760px column; the flex:1 email field absorbs all shrinkage, leaving the one field the admin must type into unusably narrow, with colliding uppercase labels and three vertical alignments in one row.

Evidence web/src/ui/Admin.tsx:461-489 (Email Field flex:1 + TTL select + Recovery select + admin check + email-it check + Invite button in one .row); web/src/ui/styles.css:173 (.row { display:flex; gap:10px } — no wrap). Screenshot verified.

Fix Let the row wrap or split it: email full-width on its own line, controls on a second row with select min-widths; give the email field min-width ~24ch.

MediumFixed px type ramp + vault rows hard-pinned to 72px — browser font-size preferences are ignored and scaling text clips inside the rowMTypography, spacing, layout & responsive

body sets no font-size so name/sub lines (font:inherit) scale with the browser preference while all explicitly-sized chrome stays fixed — at a 20px default the row content outgrows the pinned 72px row and the sub line clips. The audience most likely to raise browser font size is exactly this product's stated audience.

Evidence web/src/ui/styles.css:145/156/174/247/321/329/336 etc. (all-px ramp), :238 (.vault-list .item { height:72px; overflow:hidden }) with the clipping-acknowledgment comment at :234-237; web/src/ui/Vault.tsx:556-558 (ROW_H/LIST_GAP/STRIDE constants).

Fix Convert the ramp to rem and make row height em-based; derive the virtualizer stride by measuring the first rendered row instead of mirroring a px constant.

MediumMobile nav overflow is invisible: hidden scrollbars and no fade can hide the Admin tab entirely on phonesSTypography, spacing, layout & responsive

Below 560px the nav is a horizontally scrollable strip with its scrollbar deliberately suppressed; with 4 tabs at common phone widths the Admin tab lands off-screen with no cut glyph, fade, or scrollbar — a household admin on a phone has no discoverable path to user management.

Evidence web/src/ui/styles.css:385-387 (≤560px nav overflow-x auto with scrollbars suppressed both engines); web/src/ui/Vault.tsx:371-376 (4 nav items for admins). Screenshot verified: the clip falls in a gap between buttons so the strip looks complete.

Fix Add an edge fade-out mask or thin scrollbar, shrink tab padding so 4 tabs fit at 360px, or let the nav wrap.

MediumSign-in/enroll card runs edge-to-edge on phones — .auth-shell has zero horizontal paddingSTypography, spacing, layout & responsive

The auth shell pads only vertically, so at ≤440px the welcome/unlock/recover card — the first surface a newly invited member sees — presses against the physical screen edges with clipped corners, while every other screen keeps a 20px gutter via .wrap.

Evidence web/src/ui/styles.css:107-112 (.auth-shell padding 40px 0), :113-121 (.card); web/src/ui/Welcome.tsx:117, 139, 199, 219 and Recover.tsx (card direct children, no .wrap). Screenshot verified.

Fix Change .auth-shell padding to 40px 16px (one line).

MediumMobile vault toolbar wraps into a ragged 3-row stack with a stranded lone trash iconSTypography, spacing, layout & responsive

On a 420px phone the seven toolbar controls wrap wherever they break, splitting the create actions and stranding the trash icon on its own row; the vault list starts a full screen-quarter down and the grouping communicates no hierarchy on the product's most-used screen.

Evidence web/src/ui/styles.css:202-204 (.toolbar flex-wrap, search flex:1 1 200px); web/src/ui/Vault.tsx:493-535 (search, +Login, +Note, [+Card gated off], Import, ExportMenu, health icon, trash icon). Screenshot verified.

Fix Explicit mobile layout: search full-width, one deterministic action row, occasional tools (Import/Export/health/trash) folded into an overflow menu.

MediumGlobal uppercase label rule hits sentence-length safety-critical text — consent acknowledgments and fingerprint instructions render as multi-line letterspaced ALL CAPS (merged with the flows-cluster duplicate)STypography, spacing, layout & responsive

A caption style built for 1–3-word field labels is inherited by every checkbox acknowledgment — the longest, most consequential sentences in the product (unrecoverability, identity verification, plaintext export). Multi-line 12–13px uppercase destroys word shapes exactly where a family member must actually read before doing something irreversible.

Evidence web/src/ui/styles.css:145 (label uppercase +0.06em), :178 (.check is a <label>); Welcome.tsx:677, Admin.tsx:510, Vault.tsx:712, Sharing.tsx:980, ExportPanel.tsx:435-437; screenshots web-welcome-enroll-dark.png / web-admin-dark.png confirm rendering.

Fix Scope the transform (.check span { text-transform:none; font-size:13px } or a .label-caps utility for short captions); set sentence-length labels in normal case.

MediumInactivity auto-lock fires with zero warning and silently destroys unsaved editor workMTrust/security-UX — lock-state & data loss

A member who walks away mid-edit returns to a truthful Unlock reason line but a destroyed draft — possibly including a generated password already set on a website. Lock legibility is otherwise strong; only the warning/grace/draft-preservation is missing.

Evidence web/src/ui/useAutoLock.ts:38-46; web/src/ui/App.tsx:68-70 (the 426 comment), :194-213 (auto-lock wiring); web/src/ui/Vault.tsx:1377-1381.

Fix Pre-lock warning (~60 s out) via the persistent Announcer + a visible banner with 'Stay unlocked'; ideally preserve non-secret editor fields across the lock.

MediumLogin editor's username/password inputs carry no autocomplete suppression — the browser's own password manager interposes inside the vaultSPlatform fit — browser autofill interplay

A bare password field in a submitted form is exactly what browser heuristics target: offering to save the vault item's password into the browser store on save, and autofilling the user's own site credentials into whatever item is open — both wrong inside a zero-knowledge manager.

Evidence web/src/ui/Vault.tsx:1512 (form), :1532 (username, no autoComplete), :1541 (password, no autoComplete); contrasts at :1302, :1576, :717.

Fix autoComplete="new-password" on the item password, autoComplete="off" on the editor username; manually verify Chrome's save-password prompt stays quiet.

MediumVersion-history 'Restore' overwrites the live item in one click with no confirmation — and its success is completely silentSStates, feedback, motion & interaction quality

One tap on Restore replaces the current password immediately and dumps the user to the vault list with no confirmation anything happened — reads as 'nothing happened', or goes unnoticed when tapped accidentally.

Evidence web/src/ui/Vault.tsx:1120-1130 (restore), :967 (onRestored={onBack}), :489 (onBack = setSelected(null)).

Fix House inline-confirm ('Replace the current password with the version from ⟨date⟩?'); on success stay in Detail with a visible + announced confirmation.

MediumDisabled ghost and link buttons are visually identical to enabled ones — cursor stays pointer and hover effects still fireSStates, feedback, motion & interaction quality

During any busy state, disabled ghost/link buttons still look and hover exactly like live ones, then ignore the click — 'busy, wait' is indistinguishable from 'broken'.

Evidence web/src/ui/styles.css:165 (sole :disabled rule), :167-171 (.ghost), :279-280 (.link); disabled call-sites as listed, all verified.

Fix Add button.ghost:disabled, .link:disabled { opacity:.45; cursor:not-allowed } and neutralize :hover via :hover:not(:disabled).

MediumOffline/degraded state is a 7px colour dot with hover-only copy, and a failed manual 'Sync now' produces no visible responseSStates, feedback, motion & interaction quality

For non-technical users the degraded state is effectively invisible: the explanatory copy exists only as a desktop tooltip and an AT-only region, and pressing 'Sync now' while offline flips to 'Syncing…' and back with no failure message ever. The underlying wsUp/syncOk state machine is excellent; only its presentation is missing.

Evidence web/src/ui/Vault.tsx:380-394 (dot + title + SR twin + Sync-now), :199-208 (catch → setSyncOk(false) only), :255-262 (manualSync: busy flag only); web/src/ui/styles.css:390-393.

Fix On sustained degradation show the existing .banner idiom ('Can't reach the server — your vault is read-safe…'); give manualSync a visible failure line.

LowuseCopy never stores its timeout id — the dedupe guard is dead code, and stacked clipboard wipes are never cancelledSStates, feedback, motion & interaction quality

A small correctness bug in the feedback primitive for the app's core action: rapid re-copies truncate the confirmation, and stacked wipe timers fire regardless of what was since copied.

Evidence web/src/ui/Vault.tsx:831 (const timer = useRef), :835 (if (timer.current) clearTimeout), :836 (window.setTimeout(...) return value dropped), :838 (unconditional wipe timer).

Fix Assign timer.current = window.setTimeout(…); track and reset the wipe timer the same way.

LowLink-styled buttons used as standalone actions have ~20px touch targets (padding: 0)STypography, spacing, layout & responsive

Zero-padding text buttons are the sole affordance for back-navigation, banner dismissal, and update-reload, presenting ~20-22px hit areas — under 24px and uncomfortable on the household's phones, with 'Dismiss' sitting adjacent to flowing banner text.

Evidence web/src/ui/styles.css:279 (.link { font:inherit; padding:0 }); web/src/ui/Vault.tsx:877, 747, 1133, 1359, 701; web/src/ui/App.tsx:233.

Fix Give .link an invisible hit-area extension (padding 6px 4px + negative margins) or min-height:24px inline-flex.

LowTrash and Vault-health are dead-end screens on desktop: no back affordance, no active nav stateSFlows, information architecture & content

Both views are reached from vault-toolbar icons that vanish on arrival; no back link is offered (unlike every other layer) and no nav item highlights, so a desktop user must deduce that clicking 'Vault' is the exit.

Evidence web/src/ui/Vault.tsx:1049-1055, :342-345, :371-376, :877, :2006; web/src/ui/Health.tsx:97-106; web/src/ui/ExportPanel.tsx:267.

Fix Add '← back to vault' to both (ViewHeader could take onBack), or keep Vault highlighted while a vault-subview is open.

LowConnectivity dot's on/off states are nearly isoluminant and hue-only (1.03:1 light, 1.37:1 dark)SColor, contrast & theming

The online/offline flip is green↔grey-brown at near-identical luminance, so for red-green colorblind members the 7px dot conveys nothing but its glow. Redundant signals (Sync-now button, SR twin) keep it short of a 1.4.1 failure, but the at-a-glance function fails for ~5% of users. See also the medium-severity offline-presentation finding, which covers the sighted-user visibility gap.

Evidence web/src/ui/styles.css:198-199 (.dot.on var(--ok) + glow; .dot.off var(--ink-faint)), :193 (7px); Vault.tsx:380-394. Recomputed: #4f7a3a vs #786c50 = 1.03:1; #7fa86a vs #8d8370 = 1.37:1.

Fix Differentiate by shape: hollow ring for offline vs filled+glow for online; keep the text twin.

Extension 9

HighLight-theme input focus indicator fails non-text contrast (2.27:1) — outline suppressed, replacement border near-invisibleSColor, contrast & theming

The UA outline is removed on all inputs (email, master password, search) and the light-theme replacement — a 1px #c9a94a border plus a 14%-alpha gold halo — is far below the 3:1 non-text-contrast minimum. Keyboard users in light mode cannot reliably see focus on the unlock form of a password manager.

Evidence extension/popup.css:123 `input:focus { outline: none; border-color: var(--gold-deep); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(208,169,74,0.14) }`; light --gold-deep #c9a94a (popup.css:47). Recomputed: 2.27:1 vs #fff input bg, 1.98:1 vs #f4efe4, halo composite #f8f3e6 = 1.11:1 vs white, border delta vs #e0d8c5 = 1.60:1. Dark #8a6d28 = 3.27/3.83 passes.

Fix Use --gold #9a7420 (4.29:1 vs white, verified) or --ink for the light focus border and raise the halo alpha; better, introduce a dedicated per-theme --focus token instead of reusing --gold-deep. Add the pair to contrast.test.ts at >=3:1.

HighIn-page fill actions fail silently: dropdown pick, search-all pick, and 'Use a strong password' close the dropdown and do nothing on failureSStates, feedback, motion & interaction quality

If the reveal is refused (locked mid-flight, SW restart returning undefined via safeSend) or the SPA swapped the form so liveForm() finds nothing, the dropdown closes and the page sits unchanged with no feedback. The toast facility with live-region support already exists in the same UI layer, so the fix is wiring, not new UI.

Evidence content.ts:151-155 fillItem: `closeDropdown(); if (r?.ok && r.secret) fillForm(...)` — no else; content.ts:162 fillExplicit `if (!r?.ok || !r.secret) return;`; content.ts:175-178 useStrongPassword `if (!r) return; ... if (!live) return;`; content.ts:76 fillForm `if (!live) return;`. showToast (content-ui.ts:502) never called on any failure path.

Fix On every !ok/undefined result and on liveForm()===null, call showToast() with mapped copy per the E1 canon (locked → point at the toolbar icon; otherwise suggest the popup copy buttons).

HighPopup 'Fill this page' signals success (popup closes) even when nothing was filled — SW answers ok:true on message delivery, not fill outcomeMStates, feedback, motion & interaction quality

The detail view's primary CTA closes the popup as its success signal, but ok:true only means delivery. On pages with no detectable form (articles, dashboards, iframe-hosted logins — the grant is deliberately frame-0-only) the popup vanishes, nothing fills, and no error or toast appears — an affirmative false-success gesture.

Evidence popup.ts:465-472 `if (r?.ok) { window.close(); }`; background.ts:906-911 fillFromPopup resolves ok:true after sendMessage(tab.id, msg, {frameId:0}); content.ts:339-345 `const f = all.find(...) ?? all[0]; if (f) void fillItem(...)` — no-form → silent, no response. The only failure copy (background.ts:909) fires only when the message can't be delivered at all.

Fix Have content.ts's fillItem handler return an outcome (filled / no-form / reveal-refused) through the sendMessage response and propagate it via fillFromPopup; keep the popup open on non-fill with mapped copy naming the copy buttons as fallback, or at minimum toast 'No login form found on this page'.

MediumCard rows look clickable and promise 'Copy card details' on hover, but clicking the row does nothingSStates, feedback, motion & interaction quality

Every signal on a card row says 'click me' — pointer cursor, hover highlight, a tooltip promising an action, and the learned pattern from login rows one section above — yet clicking is a silent no-op, making the Cards feature read as broken.

Evidence popup.ts:598-625 (cardRow: r.title = 'Copy card details', no handler/role/tabIndex) vs popup.ts:241-279 (login row: role=button, tabIndex=0, click+keydown); popup.css:209 (.item { cursor: pointer }).

Fix Give card rows a click behavior (a copy-only detail view matching the login pattern) or remove the interactive costume: cursor:default, drop the misleading title, keep the copy pills always visible.

MediumRaw internal SW error strings ('locked', 'unknown item', 'no active tab', String(e)) render verbatim in the popup's red strip, violating the project's own error canonSStates, feedback, motion & interaction quality

The code-to-copy discipline applied to unlock and save was skipped for reveal/fill/card paths: an autolock racing a copy click renders the bare word 'locked' in the error strip, and a dispatch exception surfaces raw 'Error: …' text — reading as a crash to the household audience the canon exists to protect.

Evidence popup.ts:330, :470, :481-483, :631-633 — all `showMsg("err", r?.error ?? fallback)`; background.ts:827 ('locked'), :829/:897 ('unknown item'), :846 ('not allowed for this site'), :900 ('no active tab'), :187 (`error: String(e)`); errors.ts:1-8 (canon: surfaces never render raw error detail).

Fix Add reveal/fill error codes to messages.ts, map them in errors.ts (pinned by errors.test.ts), and change the four call sites to never render r?.error.

MediumClicking 'Unlock' with an empty email or password is a silent no-opSStates, feedback, motion & interaction quality

A new family member clicking the big gold Unlock button with empty fields gets nothing — no error strip, no focus jump, no button state change — the classic 'is it frozen?' moment on the very first screen.

Evidence popup.ts:694-698 (early return, no feedback); popup.html:40-48 (no required/invalid hooks); popup.css:118-127 (inputs style only :focus).

Fix Focus the first empty input and show the info strip ('Enter your email and master password to unlock.'), or disable the button until both fields are non-empty.

MediumDaily unlock friction: every 15-min idle relock costs a full online sign-in + ~6 s Argon2id; locked off-network = zero access to synced itemsLflows/IA/content

The most common journey after an idle relock is five steps plus a ~6 s KDF stall, and because unlock is a server round-trip, a locked extension off the tailnet cannot decrypt anything already synced — real friction for the household audience with no PIN/quick-unlock tier.

Evidence background.ts:52 (15-min default), :555-585 (full network unlock), :617-630 (policy fetch — already tunable server-side), :30-34 (session cleared on browser exit); popup.html:51 ('about 6 seconds'); errors.ts:25 (UNREACHABLE is the only off-network guidance).

Fix Stage it: surface/tune the idle policy per household (the server knob exists), add a quick-unlock tier (PIN-wrapped session key), and state the VPN requirement in the locked view's unreachable state.

MediumNo at-a-glance lock state on the toolbar icon — locked and unlocked-no-match look identicalStrust/security-UX

After an idle autolock the toolbar looks exactly as it did seconds before; the user cannot tell whether andvari is standing guard without opening the popup.

Evidence background.ts:175-176 (badge colors only), :508-511 (badge = count or ''), :321-328 (lock clears badges); grep: zero setIcon calls in src/.

Fix Toggle a desaturated icon set via chrome.action.setIcon in doLock/unlock, or set a global lock-glyph badge while locked.

LowServer-status dot is color-only and in light theme on/off are luminance-identical (1.03:1)SColor, contrast & theming

For red-green colorblind members the two dot states are essentially the same lightness in light mode and the glow adds no redundant channel; the at-a-glance function of the dot is lost for them, though the state is retrievable via the footer ping.

Evidence popup.css:102-104 (.dot.on --ok + same-color glow; .dot.off --ink-faint) on a 7px circle; popup.ts:61-64 (setConn); computed on-vs-off: light #4f7a3a vs #786c50 = 1.03:1, dark 1.37:1; popup.html:13-14 (visually-hidden twin covers AT only).

Fix One CSS rule: hollow/border-only dot when off (shape difference), or echo the state as text near the footer diagnostics row.

Android 19

HighNo window-insets handling while targeting SDK 35 — banner stack and non-Scaffold screens draw under system bars on Android 15+STypography, spacing, layout & responsive

Android 15 enforces edge-to-edge for targetSdk-35 apps: the non-dismissable temp-password banner and the escrow re-seal card compose above the screen switch outside any Scaffold and will render under the status bar; bottom-of-scroll controls sit behind the nav/gesture area. Scaffold-based screens are protected by their TopAppBars, exactly as the finding states.

Evidence build.gradle.kts:26 targetSdk=35. Grep across src/main: zero hits for WindowInsets/enableEdgeToEdge/statusBarsPadding/navigationBarsPadding/imePadding/safeDrawing/systemBars (verified). themes.xml:5-9 relies on statusBarColor/navigationBarColor (ignored under enforced edge-to-edge). AndvariApp shell MainActivity.kt:204-228: Surface>Column with MustChangePasswordBanner + AttentionArea as first children, no top inset. RecoverySetupScreen :586 and UnlockScreen :691 have no Scaffold and no inset padding; scroll columns end flush with the window bottom.

Fix Add windowInsetsPadding(WindowInsets.safeDrawing) (or statusBarsPadding + navigationBarsPadding/contentPadding) to the shell Column and every non-Scaffold screen; verify on an API 35 emulator in both nav modes.

HighUnlockScreen and all four autofill-overlay cards are non-scrollable centered columns — Unlock button unreachable with the IME open in landscape/split-screen/half-foldSTypography, spacing, layout & responsive

With adjustResize, a centered non-scrolling Column clips at both ends once the keyboard halves the window; the password field and/or Unlock button leave the viewport with no way to reach them, on the app's most-used screen and on overlays that appear over a form the user was already typing in.

Evidence MainActivity.kt:691 Column(fillMaxSize().padding(24.dp), Arrangement.Center) with no verticalScroll — contrast WelcomeScreen :414 which has one. Content stack :692-724 = Sigil(3 lines)+email+lockReason+ErrorBar+quickUnlockMessage+30-day notice+SecretField+Unlock+biometric button+sign-out. AutofillUnlockActivity.kt:289-293 UnlockCard same pattern; SaveConfirmActivity.kt:394, 444, 482 same. AndroidManifest.xml:60/88/96 windowSoftInputMode=adjustResize (verified). Fold is a first-class household device per MainActivity.kt:110-111 comment.

Fix Add verticalScroll to UnlockScreen's column and the four overlay cards, keeping centering only when content fits.

HighEditor 'cancel' discards all typed work in one tap while system Back shows a discard-confirm dialogSStates, feedback, motion & interaction quality

Two exits from the same editor carry contradictory guard rails: the visible top-of-form 'cancel' throws away a fully-typed login (password, TOTP secret, attachments) with no confirmation, while the code's own comment shows the data-loss class was recognized and fixed only on the Back path.

Evidence MainActivity.kt:1356 TextButton(onClick = onCancel) { Text("cancel") }; wired at :837 to onCancel = { vm.closeEditor() }. BackHandler { confirmDiscard = true } at :1310 with 'Discard changes?' AlertDialog :1311-1318 and comment :1309 'System back asks before throwing typed work away'. AndvariViewModel.kt:520-524 closeEditor() clears editorPendingUploads immediately.

Fix Route 'cancel' through the same confirmDiscard state, ideally only when the form is dirty vs `initial`.

HighOne-tap 'Sign out / use a different account' wipes local vault cache, quick-unlock, and unsynced edits with no confirmation (merged states + flows duplicates)SFlows — destructive action guard

The only unguarded destructive verb in an app that guards every other one, placed under the button a frustrated locked-out user is mashing; in a zero-knowledge client, signing back in needs the (self-hosted) server reachable, so a mistap while traveling costs all offline access plus any unsynced edits in the deleted cache DB.

Evidence MainActivity.kt:724 TextButton(onClick = vm::signOut) directly below the Unlock button — no dialog; same at :380 (upgrade screen). AndvariViewModel.kt:1895-1932: signOut revokes tokens, deleteCache (:323-325 deletes vault-<userId>.db + wal/shm), QuickUnlock.wipe, store.clear(). Contrast: item delete confirm :1221-1229, leave-vault warns 'any edits you haven't synced will be discarded' SharingScreen.kt:322-326, vault delete type-the-name SharingScreen.kt:503-518.

Fix Add a confirmation dialog in the app's existing guard-rail idiom stating the offline-access and unsynced-edit consequences.

HighNo forgot-password/recovery affordance anywhere, despite onboarding explicitly promising self-recoverySFlows — recovery/forgot-password

A locked-out family member sees no path and no pointer to web recovery — only a destructive escape. Verified literally true. Severity corrected from critical to high: recovery does exist (web + admin backstop is always sealed on the native `required` path per AndvariViewModel.kt:843-891), so this is a severe dead-end and a broken promise, not unrecoverable data loss.

Evidence UnlockScreen MainActivity.kt:680-726 offers only Unlock, biometric, and the destructive sign-out; SignInForm :431-455 has no recovery affordance; grep 'forgot' in app-android/src hits only the :571 code comment. RecoverySetupScreen :597 promises 'If you ever forget your master password, this phrase lets you recover your account yourself.' TODO(recovery-cut-2) :566-573 defers native self-recovery to web with no user-visible pointer.

Fix Add a 'Forgot your master password?' TextButton on Unlock and Sign-in opening static explainer copy pointing at web recovery / the household admin.

HighItems never show which vault they live in — personal vs shared secrets indistinguishable in list, detail, and editorMInformation architecture — vault context

The vault list interleaves personal and every shared vault into one flat list; a member editing 'Netflix' cannot tell private copy from the household-shared one — the core privacy hazard for this product class. Verified at all three surfaces.

Evidence VaultRow MainActivity.kt:1079-1101 renders initial/name/username/type only. ItemDetail :1168-1178 subtitle = 'Login'/'Card'/'Secure note' + optional ' · view only' — no vault name. Editor vault picker only for NEW items (:1267-1270, existing items get emptyList). The only surfacing of an item's vault is MoveCopyControl's move warning :628-632.

Fix Add a vault chip/suffix on VaultRow for shared items and an 'In: <vault>' line on detail and editor; optionally a vault filter above search.

HighAutofill — the product's core daily value — is never offered at onboarding and hides behind a diagnostics-framed Settings cardMFlows — daily-use setup discoverability

A newly-enrolled family member is never told autofill exists nor walked through the per-browser Trust ceremony or Chrome's setting; without it the daily flow degrades to search-and-copy. All cited framing and gating verified.

Evidence Post-enroll flow lands on RecoverySetup → vault (AndvariViewModel.kt:886-893); the only post-unlock offer is quick unlock (MainActivity.kt:214, 2094-2112). Settings card copy :1659-1662 is pure troubleshooting ('Is autofill working? … screenshot-friendly') → 'Autofill status' screen, whose 'Set as autofill service' button renders only when disabled (AutofillStatusScreen.kt:104-131). Chrome's extra 'Autofill using another service' + restart step documented only in that screen's Browser support card (:351).

Fix Add a one-time post-enrollment offer card firing ACTION_REQUEST_SET_AUTOFILL_SERVICE (mirroring QuickUnlockOfferCard) and rename the Settings card to lead with setup.

HighNo secret field declares KeyboardType.Password — IME suggestion strip and personal-dictionary learning apply to master passwords, item passwords, backup passphrases, and CVVsStrust/security-UX — secret handling

Compose communicates field type to the IME only via KeyboardOptions; a VisualTransformation masks pixels while EditorInfo still says TYPE_CLASS_TEXT, so third-party keyboards may echo the typed password in the suggestion strip and learn fragments into cloud-synced typing models — a real leak class for a zero-knowledge product. Single-composable fix.

Evidence Grep: zero occurrences of KeyboardType.Password in app-android/src (verified). SecretField MainActivity.kt:2128-2135 sets PasswordVisualTransformation but no keyboardOptions at all; overlay password fields AutofillUnlockActivity.kt:300-308 and SaveConfirmActivity.kt:489-493 likewise. Affected call sites verified: :447, :716, :483, :495, :1366, :1403, :1900-1901. Contrast: the mono Field :2119-2125 shows the team does set keyboardOptions deliberately elsewhere.

Fix Give SecretField and the two overlay fields KeyboardOptions(keyboardType = KeyboardType.Password, autoCorrectEnabled = false).

MediumOutline/border role fails 3:1 non-text contrast in both themes (1.24–1.45:1) — unfocused text-field bounds and OutlinedButton borders near-invisibleSColor, contrast & theming

WCAG 1.4.11 asks 3:1 for component boundaries; these run 1.24–1.45:1. The finder's own mitigation notes (deliberate hairline aesthetic, floating labels, gold focused border) are accurate, making medium the right rating.

Evidence Recomputed: dark #38311F vs bg #14120E = 1.45:1, vs surface #1E1B15 = 1.33:1 (Theme.kt:22,35); light #E0D8C5 vs #F4EFE4 = 1.24:1, vs #FBF8F1 = 1.34:1 (:47). Field/SecretField are OutlinedTextFields (MainActivity.kt:2119-2135) used for master password/invite/fingerprint entry; OutlinedButton sites verified ('Decline' SharingScreen.kt:140, 'Sign out / change server' :380).

Fix Raise outline to ≥3:1 against its usual backdrops, or override just text fields/OutlinedButtons via component-level color APIs while keeping the decorative card edge.

MediumLight theme ships permanently dark charcoal status/nav bars and a dark launch flash — no values-night splitSColor, contrast & theming

In light mode, system bars stay dark charcoal for the whole session on API 29–34 devices and launch flashes dark before the light UI paints — the inverse of the flash the file's comment says it prevents. (On Android 15+ the bar colors are ignored entirely — covered by the insets finding.)

Evidence res/values/themes.xml:5-9 hardcodes windowBackground/statusBarColor/navigationBarColor = #14120E; res/ contains only values/ (no values-night — verified by directory listing); grep: zero enableEdgeToEdge/window-color code in Kotlin; Compose paints light background #F4EFE4 (Theme.kt:43).

Fix Add values-night/themes.xml with the current values and re-point default themes.xml at light equivalents with windowLightStatusBar/windowLightNavigationBar, or adopt enableEdgeToEdge driven by the Compose theme.

Medium12sp bodySmall is the workhorse style — high-stakes instructional and consequence copy set at caption sizeMTypography, spacing, layout & responsive

'This account is gone forever'-class prose renders at 12sp in the dim onSurfaceVariant tone for an audience explicitly including older household members, and uniform 12sp collapses in-card hierarchy. Verified throughout.

Evidence Theme.kt:52-55 overrides only headlineMedium/titleLarge → bodySmall stays M3 12sp. Grep counts: bodySmall 136 uses vs bodyMedium 21 (finder's '~70+ vs ~15' actually understated). Verified long-paragraph sites: UnopenableVaultWarning MainActivity.kt:334-338, MustChangePasswordBanner body :252-256, vault-delete consequences SharingScreen.kt:464-501, trash explainer :1541-1545, backup tip :1920-1923, CSV plaintext warning :1974-1977; recovery posture copy is bodyMedium (:593-599) while its sibling mismatch error is bodySmall (:617-624).

Fix Re-map instructional/consequence copy to bodyMedium; reserve bodySmall for metadata and inline validation.

MediumNo large-screen adaptation: full-bleed forms, buttons, and 90-110-char lines on the Fold inner screen and tablets — one widthIn in the whole appMTypography, spacing, layout & responsive

Password fields and primary buttons stretch to the full ~674dp Fold inner width and bodySmall paragraphs exceed the readability band, while the one constrained screen proves the pattern was known. Verified.

Evidence Grep: zero WindowSizeClass/BoxWithConstraints/LocalConfiguration usage; the only width constraint is UpgradeRequiredScreen's widthIn(max = 380.dp) at MainActivity.kt:377 (both verified). WelcomeScreen :414, ItemEditor :1355, SettingsScreen :1602 are fillMaxWidth columns; PrimaryButton unconditionally fillMaxWidth (:2199). Fold-posture engineering elsewhere confirmed (:110-111, :434-435, :459-464 comments).

Fix Shared centered content-column wrapper (~480dp max) for form screens and overlays; defer two-pane.

MediumVault list (and Trash) is a non-lazy Column — every row composes eagerly and the filter re-runs per keystroke, the exact freeze class the code caps in its own dialogs (merged type-layout + states duplicates)MTypography, spacing, layout & responsive / performance

After the browser-export import the product explicitly supports, a several-hundred-item vault composes every card on first frame and re-filters synchronously per search keystroke on mid-range phones. The import dialogs were carefully capped for exactly this failure class; the destination list was not. Two findings (type-layout #8, states #7) describe the same defect and are merged.

Evidence MainActivity.kt:839-852: Column(verticalScroll) { filtered.forEach { VaultRow } }; filter computed in composition :785-795. Grep: zero LazyColumn in the app (verified). The codebase's own hazard comments verified: :981-985 'composing them all is a multi-second main-thread freeze', BUCKET_EXPAND_CAP=100 / MAX_ERROR_ROWS_SHOWN at :1050-1051. CSV import cap 10 MiB :779. TrashScreen same pattern :1560.

Fix LazyColumn with items(filtered, key=itemId) + contentPadding for vault list and Trash; derive `filtered` via remember/derivedStateOf keyed on query+items.

MediumOne global busy flag disables/spins every affordance for operations it didn't startMStates, feedback, motion & interaction quality

A slow user-initiated op anywhere makes unrelated controls grey out or falsely show in-flight spinners with no explanation — the disabled-with-no-reason pattern the codebase itself avoids elsewhere (ReSealCard stateDescription :666-670). Verified.

Evidence AndvariViewModel.kt:130-133 documents 'enable-state of every user affordance … keys off busy ONLY'. Verified consumers: PrimaryButton(busy = ui.busy) at MainActivity.kt:453, 552, 626, 718, 1451, 1700, 1722; AttachmentRow enabled=!ui.busy :1207; Trash buttons :1576-1578; export buttons :1650-1652. F74 stall comment :1282-1283 verified. PrimaryButton replaces the label with the spinner (:2200).

Fix Scope busy per operation family, or at minimum keep the label beside the spinner and exempt unrelated screens.

MediumTwo unrelated trash surfaces share the identical Delete icon with no cross-link — a vanished vault is unfindable from TrashSInformation architecture — navigation/naming

Deleted items and deleted/removed vaults live behind visually identical icons on different screens with no cross-reference from the obvious one. Verified; the finder slightly undercounted the breadcrumbs (the anomaly notice also names the location) but both are transient/dismissable, so the conclusion stands.

Evidence MainActivity.kt:806 Icons.Default.Delete opens item Trash; SharingScreen.kt:185-190 the identical Icons.Default.Delete inside Sharing discloses 'Recently deleted vaults' + 'Recently removed'. TrashScreen copy :1541-1545 and empty state :1559 mention only items. Pointers to the vault trash: owner delete notice AndvariViewModel.kt:1108 and the anomaly lifecycle notice SharingScreen.kt:90 — both transient.

Fix One 'Looking for a deleted or removed vault? Sharing → the trash icon' line in TrashScreen; consider a distinct icon for the Sharing disclosure.

MediumServer-2FA setup secret (otpauth URI + base32 seed) copied with no EXTRA_IS_SENSITIVE and no auto-clear — the one secret-class copy on the unprotected pathStrust/security-UX — clipboard consistency

A durable second-factor seed takes the deliberately-unprotected path built for public material: visible in the Android 13+ clipboard preview, eligible for cloud/cross-device clipboard sync, persisting until overwritten. The no-clear UX choice is defensible; omitting the sensitivity flag is a pure gap. Verified.

Evidence MainActivity.kt:1694-1695 SelectableCopyRow for both values; :2036 passes clearSeconds=0; :2240-2247 the <=0 branch sets a plain ClipData and returns before EXTRA_IS_SENSITIVE and the auto-clear (comment classes it 'NON-secret setup material'). Every other secret path sets the flag + clear (:2248-2264). ZXing is already a dependency (libs.versions.toml:15/56 — QR recommendation viable).

Fix Always set EXTRA_IS_SENSITIVE on these two copies, use a long (2-5 min) clear window instead of none, and prefer an in-app QR.

LowFixed dp around scalable sp content: 90dp history date column breaks at ≥1.3x font scale; AttentionArea's absolute 360dp cap can consume a landscape viewportSTypography, spacing, layout & responsive

Both spots verified; impact is narrow (large-font history rows; exceptional banner pile-ups in landscape), so low is right.

Evidence MainActivity.kt:1124: 10-char mono bodySmall date in Modifier.width(90.dp), no maxLines (wraps when scaled — math checks: ~94dp at 1.3x); :285: AttentionArea Column heightIn(max=360.dp) + verticalScroll above the weight(1f) screen Box (:215) — a full pile-up in a ~320-360dp landscape viewport leaves the list ~0dp, though the area itself scrolls (:285) so its own content stays reachable.

Fix wrapContentWidth for the date; proportional cap (e.g. 45% of maxHeight) for AttentionArea.

LowFailed Version-history and Trash loads render a literal empty string, with the explaining ErrorBar potentially scrolled off-screenSStates, feedback, motion & interaction quality

A designed-in blank failure state; the tap appears to do nothing when the error lands off-viewport. Verified; low is fair for a secondary surface.

Evidence MainActivity.kt:1119 `versions == null -> Text(if (ui.busy) "Loading…" else "")` and :1558 (Trash, same pattern) verified. Trigger 'Version history' button renders low in the detail scroll (:1113 via :1212 section placement) while ErrorBar renders at the top (:1166).

Fix Inline failure copy + retry in place of the empty string; small progress indicator while loading.

LowMain-app sign-in/unlock screens lack the autofill exclusion the overlays set — master password becomes visible to third-party autofill when Compose 1.8+ landsStrust/security-UX — defense in depth

Latent today, exactly as the finder says — the verified inconsistency against the codebase's own stated posture makes it a legitimate low with a cheap pin.

Evidence AutofillUnlockActivity.kt:125-127 and SaveConfirmActivity.kt:149-150 set IMPORTANT_FOR_AUTOFILL_NO_EXCLUDE_DESCENDANTS as documented belt-and-suspenders; grep confirms MainActivity sets none. Service self-guard AndvariAutofillService.kt:61-65 only stops andvari filling itself. compose-bom 2024.12.01 = Compose 1.7 (libs.versions.toml:17) where Compose fields don't publish to the Autofill framework; 1.8 enables it by default.

Fix Mirror the exclusion in MainActivity while on Welcome/Unlock, or pin a comment/lint on the BOM noting the 1.8 default flip.

Desktop 17

HighOne global `busy` flag couples unrelated operations: every 5-min poll and every window re-focus hijacks the editor's Save button into a spinnerMStates, feedback, motion & interaction quality

Alt-tabbing back to the app mid-edit turns the user's own Save button into an anonymous spinner and greys Cancel for the duration of a network round-trip they didn't initiate — unbounded if the server is unreachable-but-not-refusing. The same coupling briefly disables Trash/Settings/transfer buttons on every poll tick.

Evidence DesktopState.kt:981-1004 (backgroundSync: busy = true, shared flag); 1007-1010 + Main.kt:76-83 (onWindowFocus → backgroundSync); Ui.kt:2372-2377 (Primary renders CircularProgressIndicator instead of its label when busy); Ui.kt:482/1844/1736 (editor Save spinnered, Cancel disabled); DesktopState.kt:330-331 (HttpClient(Java) with no timeout config).
