Fix Give backgroundSync its own `syncing` flag that op() and UI gates ignore (overlap guard checks both); surface passive sync via a small dedicated toolbar affordance, never by repainting user buttons. Consider request timeouts on the shared client.

HighIdle auto-lock silently destroys in-progress work — an open editor's draft and the shown-once recovery phrase mid-revealMStates, feedback, motion & interaction quality

No pre-lock warning exists: typed editor fields and picked attachments vanish silently on lock (undercutting the F71 keep-the-draft investment), and a fresh enrollee who walks off to find pen and paper during the recovery reveal returns to find the phrase gone forever on this device with zero explanation.

Evidence DesktopState.kt:963-972 (guards exclude open editor/RecoverySetup); 1569-1583 (lock() zeroes secret, nulls phrase, swaps screen); Ui.kt:428-430 (editor draft = remember inside Vault, unmounted by the swap); Ui.kt:349-350 doc: 'Idle auto-lock still applies'; DesktopState.kt:1707-1715 (unlock lands in Vault, never back in RecoverySetup).

Fix Add a pre-lock warning state; defer the lock (bounded) while a dirty editor is open or preserve the draft across lock; on a lock that interrupted RecoverySetup, show an explicit notice pointing at the web re-capture path instead of silently skipping the gate.

HighThe 'un-skippable' shown-once recovery-phrase gate is silently skippable: idle auto-lock (or quit/crash) during RecoverySetup destroys the phrase and the next unlock lands straight in the vaultMStates, feedback, motion & interaction quality

The design invariant the screen claims is defeated by the app's own idle auto-lock: the member's self-service recovery is silently dead while the UI told them setup succeeded. Admin escrow (required posture) prevents total loss today and web's server-flag gate re-nudges cross-device users, but a desktop-only member gets no prompt ever — and the moment the waived posture ships this becomes an unnoticed total-loss path.

Evidence DesktopState.kt:77-81 (contract: 'Un-skippable… the silent-total-loss guard'); 1569-1583 (lock destroys the secret + phrase, screen=Unlock); 455 + 1707-1715 (unlock → Vault, never RecoverySetup); Ui.kt:349-350; DesktopState.kt:542-547 (TODO(recovery-cut-2): native vault-entry capture gate deferred); same hole for quit/crash mid-gate.

Fix On unlock/sign-in check the server's recoveryConfirmed flag and route back into a recovery-capture flow (or a blocking honest notice pointing at web/admin) when false — before the waived posture ships. Partially overlaps the idle-lock finding; this is the security-invariant half.

HighDesktop enroll cannot consume the emailed invite link — it demands a bare 'Invite token' with no guidance on where to get oneMFlows, information architecture & content

The first step of first-run onboarding asks the exact non-technical audience for dev jargon it was never given: the email carries a link, the desktop form wants a bare token plus separate email and server URL, and pasting the link is rejected with no guidance to open it in a browser instead.

Evidence Ui.kt:281 (Field("Invite token", invite, ..., mono = true) — no helper text); DesktopState.kt:497-502 (Account.enroll(inviteToken = invite...)); core EnrollLink.kt:27/100 (link shape, payload bundles origin+token+email); server EmailSender.kt:31-40 (sends the enroll link); grep EnrollLink app-desktop = 0.

Fix Run EnrollLink.parse() on the invite field's input and auto-fill token/email/server from the payload on success; failing that, relabel 'Invite code or link' with one helper line ('Paste the link from your invite email').

HighSharing screen offers no way to share: no add-member, no invite, no create-shared-vault, and no pointer to the web surface that has themSFlows, information architecture & content

The screen named 'Sharing' — where a household owner goes to share a vault — cannot share anything, and its intro copy actively describes member management it doesn't offer, failing the discoverable-without-docs bar for the core household journey.

Evidence Ui.kt:1011-1013 (doc comment); Ui.kt:1073 (copy referencing actions the screen lacks); OwnerVaultPanel Ui.kt:1188-1199 (Rename/Transfer/Delete only); grep addMember/createVault in app-desktop = none; contrast Ui.kt:98 (the app already signposts web-only features elsewhere).

Fix Add one line to the Vaults card and OwnerVaultPanel: 'To create a shared vault or add and remove members, open {baseUrl} in your browser.' Port member management longer-term.

HighNo maximum content width anywhere — a maximized window stretches every field, banner, and button edge-to-edgeSTypography, spacing, layout & responsive

The single-column layout is designed for 480dp; maximized on a 1080p/1440p monitor the master-password field, primary buttons, and body copy span the full width — 200+ character line lengths and banners whose dismiss sits far from their text. The maximized state is common on desktop and currently looks unfinished.

Evidence grep widthIn in app-desktop = exactly Ui.kt:73 and 780. Field/Secret fillMaxWidth (2324, 2334), Primary fillMaxWidth (2373), Settings/Sharing cards fillMaxWidth (1903/1918/1930/1955, 1069), banners fillMaxWidth (82, 96); Main.kt:69 rememberWindowState(480x720), freely resizable.

Fix Wrap screen content in a centered Column(Modifier.widthIn(max ≈ 560-640dp)) via one shared scaffold composable; banners can stay full-width with inner text capped.

MediumKeyboard focus on buttons, cards, and icon buttons relies on Material3's default state-layer overlay — effectively invisible in both themesMColor, contrast & themingplausible

The app deliberately supports Tab->Enter flows, but once focus leaves a text field the only focus indication is the framework's low-alpha state layer, which is a marginal tint on both the charcoal and paper themes — keyboard users navigate blind between buttons, list cards, and icon buttons.

Evidence Grep over Ui.kt/Main.kt: no `indication`, no focus border/ring modifiers, no themed focus colors; interactive elements are stock Button/TextButton/Card(onClick)/IconButton (e.g. Ui.kt:760, 1045, 2373). Text fields alone get a strong focus cue (primary border, recomputed 7.73:1 dark / 4.05:1 light). The F72 keyboard investment (Ui.kt:2289-2312, comment at 407-408 'Tab->Enter on Sign out') makes Tab traversal a first-class path.

Fix Add a visible themed focus treatment (e.g. a 2dp primary focus border via a shared Modifier or custom Indication) ensuring >=3:1 against adjacent colors in both themes; verify empirically first since the default rendering couldn't be confirmed from source.

MediumFixed sp type scale with no text-scaling path — OS accessibility text-size settings have no effectMTypography, spacing, layout & responsive

For an audience that explicitly includes older household members, 11-12sp secondary text (captions, lifecycle notices, the entire import report) cannot be enlarged short of whole-display DPI scaling.

Evidence Main.kt:48-49 pins the only custom sizes; grep Density/fontScale in app-desktop = 0 hits; heavy bodySmall/labelSmall usage for captions and helper text (e.g. the recovery-check instruction, Ui.kt:309); SettingsScreen has no text-size control.

Fix Add a text-size setting implemented as a Density(density, fontScale * userFactor) CompositionLocalProvider at the theme root, persisted in DesktopSessionStore.

MediumSix icon-only toolbar actions with no tooltips anywhere, and the same trash glyph means two different things (merged: type-layout + flows duplicates)STypography, spacing, layout & responsive

Six unlabeled glyphs are the entire top-level navigation for a non-technical audience; hover — desktop's cheapest affordance — reveals nothing, FileUpload-as-import and Group-as-sharing are not self-evident, and the Delete glyph means item-Trash on one screen and deleted-vaults disclosure on the adjacent one, with system copy institutionalizing the ambiguity.

Evidence Ui.kt:457-462 (FileUpload/Group/Delete/Settings/Refresh/Lock IconButtons, contentDescription only); grep Tooltip in app-desktop = 0 hits; Ui.kt:1044-1051 second Delete glyph = 'recently deleted and removed vaults'; DesktopState.kt:868 notice copy: '(Sharing → the trash icon)'.

Fix Wrap the toolbar IconButtons in material3 TooltipBox reusing the contentDescription strings; give the Sharing disclosure a distinct icon (Restore/History) and name it in the notice copy.

MediumManual sync (Refresh) gives no feedback at all — no spinner, no disabled state, no success message — and repeat clicks stack concurrent syncsSStates, feedback, motion & interaction quality

A user syncing to pick up a partner's new password gets no visible response unless the sync fails; the natural repeated clicking launches concurrent syncs. Loading feedback exists elsewhere, so this reads as an unfinished corner.

Evidence Ui.kt:461 (IconButton(onClick = { state.refresh() }), no gating); DesktopState.kt:648 (refresh sets no notice); 1739-1741 (op(): busy = true with no re-entry guard); no last-synced display anywhere despite store.lastSyncAt (DesktopSessionStore:88-90).

Fix Bind the Refresh icon to enabled = !busy with a small spinner while running; set a success notice or a quiet 'last synced HH:MM' caption; add `if (busy) return` at the top of op().

MediumStartup blocks the UI thread on a synchronous update-check HTTP call — the static Loading rune can be a genuinely frozen window for up to ~8sSStates, feedback, motion & interaction quality

With a slow or unreachable server the first frame a fresh installer sees is an unresponsive window for up to ~8 seconds, and even off the blocked path the Loading state has no motion or copy to distinguish 'connecting' from 'hung'.

Evidence DesktopState.kt:344 (runCatching { checkForUpdate(...) } inside scope.launch on the Compose UI scope, Main.kt:64); Platform.kt:64-73 (HttpClient.send — blocking — with Duration.ofSeconds(4) x2); Ui.kt:119 (Loading = static rune, no spinner or copy). The adjacent policy probe (336) is a proper ktor suspend, so the fix pattern is in-file.

Fix Wrap checkForUpdate in withContext(Dispatchers.IO) (one line); add an indeterminate spinner and, after ~2s, a quiet 'reaching your server…' line to the Loading state.

MediumVersion-history Restore is a one-click, unconfirmed overwrite of the live item that then silently navigates awaySStates, feedback, motion & interaction quality

Every other consequential action earns a confirmation, but Restore replaces the live item on a single click adjacent to Show/Hide toggles, then pops to the list with zero statement of what changed. The overwritten version does enter the 10-deep history, but nothing tells the user that.

Evidence Ui.kt:1502 (TextButton Restore → state.saveItem(item.itemId, v.doc) { onRestored() }); Ui.kt:1585 (onRestored = onBack, silent); saveItem → refreshItems sets no notice (DesktopState.kt:613-637, 1700-1705); confirms exist for delete/purge/vault-delete.

Fix Confirm with the existing dialog idiom ('Restore the version from July 3? The current version stays in history.') and set a notice on success instead of navigating silently.

MediumLinux (.deb) build has no screen-reader support at all; Windows requires Java Access Bridge to be manually enabledLassistive-tech/platformplausible

If the platform claims hold (they match CMP's documented state), the surface's careful semantics reach no assistive tech by default on either shipped target: Orca sees nothing on the Deb, and NVDA/JAWS see nothing on Windows until a user runs jabswitch — a step no non-technical household member will discover.

Evidence app-desktop/build.gradle.kts (targetFormats(TargetFormat.Msi, TargetFormat.Deb)); a11ydesk-01..08 semantics work throughout Ui.kt (e.g. 2338, 2376); the JAB/NSAccessibility/no-Linux claim is external framework knowledge.

Fix Verify against the shipped CMP version, then document the JAB-enable step on the downloads page and state explicitly that screen-reader users should use the web vault; track CMP Linux a11y upstream.

Medium'Sign out / use a different account' on the Unlock screen is one un-confirmed click that revokes the session and deletes the local cache — including any unsynced offline editsStrust/destructive-actions

To a household member 'use a different account' sounds like a harmless switcher and sits directly under the Unlock button they're fumbling around; one click irreversibly revokes tokens, kills offline unlock, and destroys any queued unsynced edits with no confirmation.

Evidence Ui.kt:423; DesktopState.kt:1586-1622 (logout + deleteCache + store.clear); 956-961 (durable queue lives in the cache); Ui.kt:1165-1181 (LeaveControl's precedent dialog: 'any edits you haven't synced will be discarded').

Fix Add a confirm dialog matching the LeaveControl idiom: name the account, state that this device's offline copy and unsynced changes are discarded, require an explicit press.

MediumServer field accepts plain http:// (and any string) with no cleartext warningStrust/anti-spoofing

The enroll ceremony defends hard against a hostile server, yet the transport question is unguarded: a typo'd or dictated http:// URL sends auth keys and bearer tokens (replayable session credentials) over cleartext with no warning or downgrade indicator, and nothing on Unlock shows the scheme/host.

Evidence Ui.kt:2364-2369 (ServerField); DesktopState.kt:351-353 (trim + removeSuffix('/') only); DesktopSession.kt:122-123 (https tailnet default; legacy http LAN default).

Fix Show a persistent warning under the field for non-localhost http:// URLs and require explicit acknowledgment; consider surfacing scheme+host on the Unlock screen.

MediumTheme is hard-bound to OS detection with no in-app control; Linux (Deb) users are stuck in the weaker light themeMColor, contrast & theming

The dark theme is the measurably higher-quality one while light carries the documented AA failures, yet users have no choice: the app follows OS detection only, and Compose desktop's detection resolves to light on Linux desktops regardless of DE theme. Compounds the light-primary contrast finding for the entire Deb audience.

Evidence Main.kt:55 (colorScheme = if (isSystemInDarkTheme()) DarkColors else LightColors — only selection); grep over app-desktop finds zero theme-preference state; SettingsScreen (Ui.kt:1897-1990) has no theme setting; app-desktop/build.gradle.kts targetFormats(Msi, Deb).

Fix Add a System/Dark/Light setting persisted in DesktopSessionStore prefs and feed it into AndvariDesktopTheme; default System with Dark fallback when detection is unavailable.

LowSuccess/positive states reuse brand gold (secondary), so confirmation moments are visually indistinguishable from ordinary accentsSColor, contrast & theming

There is no success role: verification-passed and enrollment-confirmed states render in a gold nearly identical to the ubiquitous brand gold also used for neutral notices, muddying the semantic channel while danger gets its own color. Text carries the meaning, so this is hierarchy polish, not an accessibility failure.

Evidence secondary = #E8C66A dark / #7D5E14 light (Main.kt:37, 43) colors success ('matches' Ui.kt:315, 'Enrolled.' 2262), info (NoticeBar 162, needs-update 528), and live data (TOTP code 1888) alike, beside primary-gold buttons/badges on the same screens.

Fix Introduce a distinct success token (verdigris/moss green, >=4.5:1 on both surfaces) reserved for verification-passed and enrollment-confirmed states.

Server & email 7

HighInvite-email dispatch is completely feedback-free — sends, per-recipient rate-limit skips, compose failures, and transport failures all produce an identical success responseMstates / failure-feedback (merged: flows-content)

The one asynchronous, failure-prone action on this surface — emailing the invite — has identical UI outcomes whether the mail was dispatched, suppressed by the 5/hour per-recipient cap, never composed, or refused by the relay. The canonical support loop ('she never got it' → resend) hits the cap silently on the 6th resend within an hour; a sustained transport failure (expired Graph secret, relay down) is invisible except as a class-only Loki line. The A3/A4 rationale justifies asynchrony and PII-free logs, not zero feedback.

Evidence App.kt:649-663 (silent cap-skip, silent null-link skip, fire-and-forget send, class-only warn); core/.../model/Wire.kt:488 (no email status in InviteResponse); AdminService.kt:155-area AdminStatus.emailConfigured only; web/src/ui/Admin.tsx:490-495 ('andvari will also email the enroll link…' with no delivery feedback).

Fix The skip decisions at App.kt:652-656 are synchronous and known before respond: add an emailStatus field to InviteResponse (queued | skipped_rate_limited | skipped_unconfigured | link_error) so the Admin UI can say 'email on its way' vs 'NOT sent — share the link yourself'. For async transport failures, record a lastInviteEmailErrorAt meta/counter surfaced in AdminStatus.

MediumStatic pipeline sends zero cache headers and no compression — defeating the deliberate immutable-hash bundle design and leaving index.html open to stale-hash strandingsSplatform-fit / states (merged)

The build emits content-hashed immutable bundles but the server never says so: with no validators, browsers re-fetch the entire uncompressed bundle (libsodium-sumo + React) on effectively every visit — repeating the blank-boot window daily — while nothing marks index.html no-cache, so a heuristically-cached shell can reference purged hashes after a redeploy and render the permanent blank page from the shell finding.

Evidence App.kt:178-180 (respondBytes, no validators); App.kt:745-763 (CSP only); grep server/src/main for Compression|CachingHeaders|Cache-Control = zero hits; vite.config.ts:27-30 ('Immutable versioned bundles' comment, [hash] filenames).

Fix In the static route: `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable` for /assets/*, `Cache-Control: no-cache` for index.html/the fallback, and install Ktor's Compression plugin (gzip for text/js/wasm). ~15 lines.

MediumNo way to see or revoke outstanding invites — a mis-addressed or photographed invite is an unrevokable bearer credential for up to 72 hoursMflows-content

An admin who typos an address, invites the wrong person, or watches a QR get photographed has no in-product recourse — they cannot see the invite exists, whether it was redeemed, or when it expires (token/expiry shown once at mint). A live bearer credential to the household vault can sit unrevokable up to 72 h; the code names the TTL clamp as the sole containment.

Evidence AdminService.kt:52-55 ('NO invite list/revoke surface … SOLE containment'); App.kt:627-668 (create-only invite routes); web/src/ui/Admin.tsx ~553 ('it can't be revoked' warning copy); Janitor.kt:192 (stale comment referencing a nonexistent live-invite list).

Fix Add GET /api/v1/admin/invites (pending: email, expiresAt, usedAt, escrowPolicy) and a DELETE/revoke endpoint, surfaced as a small 'pending invitations' list with Revoke in Admin. Also gives the email-status finding its natural home.

MediumFirst-run dead-end: an expired unused bootstrap invite silently blocks re-minting for up to 30 daysSflows-content

If the operator sets ANDVARI_BOOTSTRAP_TOKEN, boots, and doesn't enroll within 72 hours, the token dies and nothing explains why: restarting with the token set is a silent no-op, the earlier boot-log claim is now stale, and the state self-heals only when the janitor prunes the row a month later.

Evidence App.kt:127 (boot call), App.kt:183-195 (userCount guard, `existing > 0` early return before the mint+println, 72 h expiry on insert); Janitor.kt:192-195 + :321 (dead invites pruned 30 d past expiry); Service.kt:132 (invite_expired).

Fix In seedBootstrap, when userCount==0, delete (or ignore) expired-unused invite rows before deciding, and log either 'bootstrap invite still valid until …' or 'expired bootstrap invite replaced'. One conditional plus a log line.

LowTheme is OS-locked end-to-end — no user override hook in the shell or token architectureMcolor

Both themes are complete and AA-tuned but the user cannot choose: shared family PCs and members who never change OS defaults have no way to pick the variant they read best, and the token structure makes a future toggle a restructure rather than a UI addition.

Evidence web/src/ui/styles.css:38 (light palette media-query-only); web/index.html:6 (static color-scheme meta); grep web/src/ui/App.tsx + Settings.tsx for theme/data-theme = zero hits.

Fix Make the light block reachable via both the media query and `:root[data-theme="light"]` (plus a dark override), stamp data-theme from a persisted setting, sync style.colorScheme; then a system/dark/light Settings toggle is trivial.

LowOnly CSP is set — no X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, or Permissions-Policy on any response, and CSP covers only the SPA file handlerStrust-security

Standard companion hardening headers are absent everywhere; any scanner, pentest, or curious family techie will flag 'password manager missing baseline security headers' first, even though the concrete exploit surface on a private origin is small.

Evidence App.kt:753-760 (CSP on webDir responses only); grep server/src/main for nosniff|Referrer|Permissions|DefaultHeaders = zero hits; App.kt:316-324 (/downloads bare).

Fix Install Ktor DefaultHeaders (or a small intercept): X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Referrer-Policy: no-referrer, deny-by-default Permissions-Policy on every response; keep the CSP as-is.

LowPublic break-glass origin has no robots exclusion — /robots.txt falls through to the SPA as 200 HTML (allow-all) and the shell carries no noindexSprivacy-discoverability

When the public origin is enabled, crawlers receive an effectively rule-free robots response and an index-permitted shell; a family vault's login page appearing in search results invites credential-stuffing and phishing-kit attention and is a raw trust hit.

Evidence web/public/ (no robots.txt); web/index.html:3-8 (no robots meta); App.kt:745-763 (/robots.txt → index.html 200 text/html); Config.kt publicHostname + isPublicOrigin support.

Fix Add web/public/robots.txt (`User-agent: *` / `Disallow: /`) plus `<meta name="robots" content="noindex,nofollow">` (harmless on the private origin); optionally X-Robots-Tag when call.isPublicOrigin().

Findings by surface — run 1131

Filter:

HighFive hand-synced copies of the design system with no shared seam — the SSOT is a comment, and drift has already shippedMcross-platform design-system consistency

The design language is physically duplicated across 5 disjoint files in 3 languages (CSS, TS template-string, Kotlin) with nothing mechanically coupling them. This is the root cause of every divergence below (F2 overlay AA lag, F3 gold-link AA fail, F4 native light gaps, F6 onPrimary mismatch). For a password manager whose brand promise is one trustworthy 'treasury' surface across web/extension/mobile/desktop, uncontrolled drift erodes the anti-spoof/recognizability value the team explicitly designed for (content-ui.ts:13).

Evidence Same 22 tokens x2 themes re-declared verbatim across web/src/ui/styles.css:5-65, extension/popup.css:6-56, extension/src/content-ui.ts:22-59 (--anv-*), app-android/.../Theme.kt:16-49, app-desktop/.../Main.kt:31-46. No import/codegen. web/src/ui/contrast.test.ts:16 reads only styles.css; extension/src/contrast.test.ts:31 reads only popup.css; each asserts only its own file's AA — neither asserts the copies are EQUAL. So copies can silently diverge as long as each independently clears AA (or isn't covered).

Fix Ship both, in order. (1) INTERIM: one node lockstep test parsing all five files (hex regexes + Kotlin Color(0xFF..) form), normalizing to a canonical {dark,light} map, asserting every shared token matches — encoding the two legitimate remaps (overlay --anv-bg == web --bg-raised; native = documented subset) and extending the ratio helper to AA-check overlay tokens (closes the F2 blind spot). Fail CI on mismatch. (2) REAL FIX: a canonical design/tokens.json + build-tokens.mjs that GENERATES web tokens.css, the popup block, extension tokens.generated.ts, and AndvariColors.kt (android+desktop schemes); CI runs `node build-tokens.mjs && git diff --exit-code`; contrast test runs once over tokens.json for all surfaces. A ~120-line script keeps the self-contained/no-dep posture.

MediumPopup and overlay color interactive text with raw gold and never adopted web's AA-tuned --link token → link text fails WCAG AA in light mode, untestedSaccessibility / token parity

The same interactive-text-in-gold pattern web deliberately darkened for AA is shipped un-darkened on popup and overlay, so light-theme users get link/action text failing AA, with no test guarding it.

Evidence Web added --link (styles.css:26 dark #d0a94a==--gold; :55 light #7d5e14, distinct from --gold #9a7420), pinned at contrast.test.ts:71-72. Popup .link (popup.css:305) and .site-link (:255) use var(--gold); overlay .row.action (content-ui.ts:109) uses var(--anv-gold). Light gold #9a7420 on popup --bg #f4efe4 ≈3.74:1 and on overlay --anv-bg #fbf8f1 ≈4.05:1 — both < AA 4.5:1. Popup contrast test covers only --ink-faint/--ink-dim (lines 60-79); overlay has no contrast test. (Note: the finding's overlay citation '.site-link content-ui.ts:255' is a misattribution — the real overlay usage is .row.action:109; .site-link:255 is popup.css.)

Fix Add --link / --anv-link to popup.css and content-ui.ts (dark=gold, light=#7d5e14) and point .link/.site-link/.row.action at it, mirroring web. Cover link tokens for all three web-family surfaces in the F1 contrast test.

MediumNative light color scheme silently drops surfaceVariant + onSurfaceVariant → branded muted text renders in Material default grey (off-brand) in light modeScross-platform design-system consistency

A dark/light asymmetry means the light theme leaks Material's stock purple-grey into the treasury aesthetic on branded copy across the whole app. Dark mode looks correct so it's easy to miss in review — the second independent instance of the hand-sync model failing. (Purely aesthetic drift; the fallback grey still clears AA.)

Evidence Theme.kt dark surfaceVariant=#262119 (:33), onSurfaceVariant=#A79C85=--ink-dim (:34); light (:39-49) omits BOTH. Same asymmetry in Main.kt (dark :39 has them, light :42-46 does not). onSurfaceVariant drives real brand text: tagline 'the keeper of the hoard' (MainActivity.kt:353, Ui.kt:140), empty-state 'Your hoard is empty.' (MainActivity.kt:849, Ui.kt:533), and ~35 further muted labels. In light those fall back to Material3's default onSurfaceVariant (~#49454F purple-grey) instead of --ink-dim #6b6047; surfaceVariant containers get Material's ~#E7E0EC instead of the warm input tone.

Fix Add surfaceVariant (#FBF8F1) and onSurfaceVariant (#6B6047=--ink-dim) to both light schemes (Theme.kt:39-49, Main.kt:42-46). Once AndvariColors.kt (F1) generates the schemes, dark/light symmetry is guaranteed by construction.

MediumBrand sigil rendering strategy diverges — web draws tofu-proof SVG geometry; popup, overlay and native render the raw runic codepoints ᛅ/ᛝMbrand-mark consistency / rendering robustness

The one place a strong tofu-proofing decision was made (web SVG) is the only place it was applied; the other four surfaces re-introduce the exact failure mode the SVG was built to prevent, so the primary brand mark can silently box out on the surfaces most exposed to unknown font environments (native, overlay on arbitrary sites), weakening brand recognition and the documented anti-spoof rationale.

Evidence web/src/ui/Sigil.tsx draws BrandSigil ᛅ / EmptySigil ᛝ as inline SVG paths, commenting the runic codepoints 'render as tofu wherever no installed font covers the Runic block, and the CSP posture bars shipping one.' Every other surface prints the codepoint as text: popup.html:10/36 + popup.ts:228 (`sigil.textContent='ᛅ'`), content-ui.ts:274 & :507 (`span('sigil','ᛅ')`), native MainActivity.kt:351/849 and Ui.kt:138/533 (`Text('ᛅ'/'ᛝ')`). Native uses generic FontFamily.Serif (Theme.kt:51 → Noto Serif on Android, no Runic coverage), and content-ui.ts:13 relies on the 'distinctive treasury surface' as an anti-spoof signal.

Fix Port the Sigil geometry everywhere: emit the two SVG paths from popup.ts/content-ui.ts (swap textContent 'ᛅ' for an inline <svg>), and on native draw the same paths with Compose Canvas/Icon instead of Text('ᛅ'). Keep the path data in one place (shared constant or generated alongside tokens.json).

LowOverlay --anv-ink-faint is stuck at the pre-AA value on both themes, and the overlay is covered by NO contrast testScross-platform design-system consistency

Concrete proof the hand-sync model already broke: the a11yweb-03/'A1 7a' fix landed on web+popup but the third web-family copy was missed, and with zero overlay contrast coverage the miss was never caught. Latent today (dead token) but a loaded gun — the moment anyone styles an overlay muted element with var(--anv-ink-faint) the extension ships sub-AA text on arbitrary sites.

Evidence content-ui.ts:30 `--anv-ink-faint: #6f6752;` (dark) and :51 `--anv-ink-faint: #9a8f74;` (light) are the OLD values web+popup abandoned for AA (styles.css:19/48 & popup.css:20/44 = #8d8370 dark / #786c50 light). #6f6752 on overlay base #1e1b15 ≈3.3:1; #9a8f74 on #fbf8f1 ≈2.8:1 — both fail AA. No test reads content-ui.ts. Mitigating (verified): grep shows --anv-ink-faint is defined-but-UNUSED in the overlay, so no live text renders sub-AA today.

Fix Bump content-ui.ts:30 to #8d8370 and :51 to #786c50 to match web/popup, then fold the overlay into the F1 lockstep + AA test so it can never drift or regress silently. Cheap to fix even though currently unused.

LowPrimary-button corner radius is three different values across surfaces despite a shared 10px radius tokenScross-platform design-system consistency

The 10px house radius is honored inconsistently: the overlay bypasses its own token with magic numbers and native never sees the token (no Compose Shapes override). Minor visually but exactly the drift the token was meant to prevent.

Evidence web button.primary border-radius:var(--radius)=10px (styles.css:160); popup button.primary var(--radius)=10px (popup.css:131); overlay button.primary hardcodes border-radius:8px (content-ui.ts:150) and .ghost 8px (:162) though --anv-radius:10px is defined (:39) and used at :74/:135; overlay search-input 7px (:121). Native has no Shapes override (grep-confirmed), so Buttons take Material3's default radius. Net: the 'same' primary button is 10px on web/popup, 8px in the overlay, fully-rounded on native.

Fix Point overlay button/ghost/search radii at var(--anv-radius) (or a deliberate --anv-radius-sm if 8px is intended). On native add a Shapes override to MaterialTheme (small=10.dp) so buttons/cards match. Route through tokens.json.

LowNative onPrimary in light mode is pure white; web's equivalent --btn-ink is warm off-white #fdf9f0Stoken parity

A small warm-vs-pure-white mismatch on primary-button text in light mode — barely perceptible in isolation but one more uncontrolled divergence and trivially avoidable.

Evidence Theme.kt:41 and Main.kt:43 light onPrimary = Color.White (#FFFFFF); web/popup light --btn-ink = #fdf9f0 (styles.css:60, popup.css:50), a warm paper white. Dark matches exactly (#1A1509 == --btn-ink dark); only light diverges.

Fix Set native light onPrimary = Color(0xFFFDF9F0) to match --btn-ink. Auto-resolved once AndvariColors.kt is generated from tokens.json.

LowTypography diverges: native uses generic Serif/Monospace families and overrides only 2 type roles; the overlay abridges the curated serif/mono stacksStypography consistency

The intent (serif for display, mono for secrets) is preserved everywhere but the actual typefaces differ by platform and the overlay ships a shorter fallback chain. Low impact since fonts must be system fonts anyway, but display serif and secret mono look materially different web vs native.

Evidence Web/popup: serif = 'Iowan Old Style','Palatino Linotype',Palatino,'Book Antiqua',Georgia,serif and mono = 'SF Mono','JetBrains Mono','Fira Code',ui-monospace,'Cascadia Code',monospace (styles.css:6-8). Overlay abridges: content-ui.ts:36 drops 'Palatino Linotype'/'Book Antiqua'; :38 drops 'Fira Code'/'Cascadia Code'. Native uses generic FontFamily.Serif (Theme.kt:51) and per-widget FontFamily.Monospace (13x in MainActivity.kt), never the curated mono; AndvariType overrides only headlineMedium+titleLarge (Theme.kt:52-55, Main.kt:47-50), leaving all body/label roles at Material defaults.

Fix Restore the full serif/mono stacks in the overlay (copy from styles.css or generate). On native, accept the generic-family constraint but document it as intentional and define a mono TextStyle token so 'secrets in mono' is a theme decision rather than scattered per-widget FontFamily.Monospace. Keep the two type roles in tokens.json.

NitOverlay token names invert the surface hierarchy (--anv-bg = the RAISED tone), a copy-by-name trap; contrast tests never assert cross-file equalitySmaintainability / drift-risk

A latent sync hazard: the one place the overlay legitimately remaps semantics is undocumented at the token site and unguarded by tests, so a well-meaning 'keep in sync' edit could invert the overlay's background/foreground.

Evidence content-ui.ts:23 `--anv-bg: #1e1b15;` (web's --bg-RAISED) and :24 `--anv-bg-deep: #14120e;` (web's --bg) — so --anv-bg != --bg by name. A maintainer syncing by matching token NAMES would swap the two surfaces. Compounding: web/src/ui/contrast.test.ts and extension/src/contrast.test.ts each validate only their own file's AA and never compare copies, so no test would catch such a swap.

Fix Add a comment at content-ui.ts:23-24 stating the deliberate remap (--anv-bg = web --bg-raised), and encode the remap explicitly in the F1 lockstep test so the relationship is machine-checked rather than tribal knowledge.

React 18 + TS + Vite — the reference implementation, and the only surface with an AA-tuned link token, SVG brand geometry, and a contrast test.

HighLight-mode focus indicator is far below 3:1 — inputs & table rows use --gold-deep at ~2:1SAccessibility (WCAG 1.4.11/2.4.11)

Keyboard and low-vision users cannot see which field/row is focused in light mode anywhere. The most impactful a11y issue on the surface.

Evidence styles.css:152-155 (input:focus border-color:var(--gold-deep), outline:none), :334 (rowlink :focus-visible outline var(--gold-deep)); light --gold-deep=#c9a94a (:51). Recomputed: 1.98/2.14/2.27:1.

Fix Use a dedicated --focus token clearing 3:1 in both themes (dark ~#e8c66a / light ~#7d5e14) as a 2px outline with outline-offset:2px, and add a real input :focus-visible rule.

MediumLight theme desaturates the gold to olive-brown and inverts the primary-button gradient sheenMpalette execution / brand expression

Light-mode primary buttons/coins/wordmark render in dark yellow-brown golds and the button's top-lit sheen inverts vs dark mode, so light reads as a lower-quality variant. The 'muddy' complaint is subjective and partly the intended AA trade-off, but the gradient-lightness inversion is a concrete defect.

Evidence styles.css:49-55 (light --gold #9a7420, --gold-bright #7d5e14) + styles.css:161 button.primary linear-gradient(180deg,var(--gold-bright),var(--gold)). Gold-bright(#7d5e14) darker than gold(#9a7420) in light → gradient top darker than bottom; opposite ordering in dark.

Fix Drive the gradient from a single --gold-hi/--gold-lo pair whose lightness ordering is theme-correct (lighter stop always on top), and keep a genuinely golden fill for the button/coin while reserving #7d5e14 for the --link/text token where AA is required.

MediumUnreadable TOTP renders as a fake, copyable gold 'invalid' code inside a full countdown ring [merges 3 findings]Sstate color semantics / trust-UX

A parse failure is painted exactly like a live code — big gold mono, full ring — and clicking copies the literal string 'invalid'. Reads as a rendering bug and could be pasted into a 2FA prompt.

Evidence Vault.tsx:1317, 1336-1338; styles.css:269 (.totp 24px gold mono), :271-273 (.ring). Reachable via imported/other-client otpauth values only.

Fix Render the failure as inert muted text ('Couldn't read this one-time code — edit the item to fix it'), suppress the ring, and never wire onCopy to a non-numeric value.

Mediumcontrast.test.ts gives false green — it only checks --ink-faint and --linkSAccessibility / test-coverage

The regression guard can't see the tokens already failing, so a11y 'green' is misleading.

Evidence web/src/ui/contrast.test.ts:58, 68-73; missing --gold-deep/--btn-ink/--gold/--ok cases (all recomputed below 4.5 or below 3:1).

Fix Extend the test to every foreground token over both surfaces (4.5:1 text), UI/focus pairs at 3:1, and the primary-button case against the gold gradient's light stop; fail the build on any miss.

MediumBusy/submit states aren't announced and disabling the button drops keyboard focusMAccessibility (WCAG 4.1.3)

SR users get no spoken feedback during multi-second crypto waits, and keyboard focus is lost when the button disables.

Evidence Welcome.tsx:153; App.tsx:228-229; Vault.tsx:2101; Health.tsx:103. No aria-busy anywhere.

Fix Add aria-busy on the form, pipe busy labels through a role=status announcer, and keep the button focusable (spinner-in-label instead of disabled, or move focus deliberately).

MediumThe shown-once recovery-phrase confirm input has no programmatic labelSAccessibility (WCAG 1.3.1/3.3.2/4.1.2)

On the recovery-phrase capture gate the confirm field lacks a persistent accessible name; a weak placeholder fallback partly mitigates the 'blank' claim but the association is still missing.

Evidence Welcome.tsx:741-752 (label without htmlFor, input without id/aria-label, placeholder present); Field.tsx:33-38 (the useId association it should use).

Fix Wrap the input in <Field label=…> or add id+htmlFor / aria-label.

MediumMulti-second Argon2id unlock/change-password has no progress affordance [merges 2 findings]MUX / perceived responsiveness

The primary web surface shows a frozen-looking gold button for several seconds (change-password ~2× KDF) with no motion, weaker than the extension popup which has an animated KDF bar.

Evidence Welcome.tsx:153,370,578; Settings.tsx:267-268,306; App.tsx:246; no @keyframes/spinner in styles.css.

Fix Add an indeterminate/animated bar (reuse the popup's) in the .primary busy state plus aria-busy; even a 'this takes a few seconds' subline helps.

MediumEnroll consent over-states the requirement — tells every invitee they need a printed recovery sheetSUX microcopy

The first onboarding screen makes a printed recovery sheet sound mandatory, but emailed invites are waived (no sheet) and QR invites verify against the admin's screen; an invitee lacking a sheet may stall.

Evidence Welcome.tsx:197,207,394,614-621; enrollposture.ts:40-43 (linkRfp→required-affirm else waived).

Fix Make the consent copy conditional on the resolved posture, or soften to 'you may be asked to confirm a recovery code from the person who invited you.'

MediumClipboard auto-clear is invisible, and Settings copies of the TOTP seed never auto-clear at all [merges 2 findings]Mtrust-UX / clipboard hygiene

The 30s wipe is never communicated (reads as data loss), and the exact secrets that most warrant it (TOTP seed/otpauth URI in Settings) get no wipe at all.

Evidence Vault.tsx:829-840,844; Settings.tsx:109-117,101,218,225; Welcome.tsx:718.

Fix Fold the window into the flash ('copied ✓ · clears in 30s'), announce the clear, and route Settings' sensitive copies through the auto-clearing copy path.

LowNo favicon, theme-color, or social meta; the ᛅ mark never appears once signed in [merges 3 findings]Siconography / first-impression

A pinned/bookmarked tab shows the generic globe and mobile chrome gets no charcoal/gold, and the rune mark is absent from every signed-in screen. Real gap but cosmetic and cheap; the visual-lens 'high' rating is inflated for a self-contained one-line fix.

Evidence web/index.html:3-7 (no icon/theme-color/OG); web/public/.well-known/assetlinks.json only; Sigil.tsx BrandSigil usages = App.tsx:245, Welcome.tsx:120/142/202/222, Recover.tsx:118 (never Vault appbar).

Fix Add a data:image/svg+xml favicon from BrandSigil, a <meta name=theme-color> per scheme (#14120e / #f4efe4), a PNG apple-touch-icon, and minimal OG tags; optionally place the small sigil beside the appbar wordmark.

LowVault toolbar mixes an all-text action language with two bare line-icons (health squiggle + trash)Siconography / visual consistency

The two most iconographic elements are also the least self-evident (the pulse squiggle doesn't obviously mean 'password health'). Consistency nit rather than a functional problem.

Evidence Vault.tsx:512-535 (Health path 'M22 12h-4l-3 9L9 3l-3 9H2' + trash outline, both aria-labelled) vs text buttons at :495-508.

Fix Give the two the same text-button treatment as neighbors, or adopt a small consistent icon set; if kept iconic, use a shield-check for health rather than a medical pulse.

LowDetail action row is unbalanced — flex:1 Edit vs right-floated Delete, Move/Copy orphaned belowSspacing / alignment

A luminous half-width gold Edit dominates a read-only view and Move/Copy read as a detached second row.

Evidence Vault.tsx:983-985, 1209-1211; styles.css:276-278 (.actions .primary flex:1 + .spacer flex:1).

Fix Size Edit to content/min-width instead of flex:1 and group Move/Copy with the primary action cluster.

LowPrimary button label allegedly fails AA on the lighter-gold half of its light-mode gradientSAccessibility (WCAG 1.4.3)plausible

Only the very bottom of the light-mode gold gradient (below the text) dips under 4.5:1; the label itself renders at ~4.7:1. A real fragility for a gradient CTA but not a clear as-shipped failure.

Evidence styles.css:158-161; recomputed gradient contrast: t=0.4→4.97, 0.5→4.81, 0.6→4.68, 0.7→4.53, 1.0→4.08 (btn-ink vs interpolated bg).

Fix Flip the gradient so the darker gold-bright anchors the bottom, or use a solid gold-bright fill in light mode; add the button case to the contrast test.

Low--gold (and --ok) as small text fall below AA in light modeSAccessibility (WCAG 1.4.3)

Several small informational texts use gold/green directly (some via inline style) that read pale and sub-AA in light mode.

Evidence Vault.tsx:359, 1565; styles.css:257, 268, 316; Health.tsx:208; Welcome.tsx:894. Ratios recomputed 3.74/4.05/4.38.

Fix Introduce a --gold-text token ≥4.5:1 on both surfaces (use --gold-bright in light) and reserve --ok for large text; replace the inline color at Vault.tsx:359 with a class.

LowEditor password-strength bar has no text or ARIA valueSAccessibility (WCAG 1.1.1/4.1.2)

The editor conveys password strength by color+width alone with no programmatic name/value.

Evidence Vault.tsx:1641-1648 (no role/aria/text; colors[0]==colors[1]==--danger).

Fix Add the STRENGTH_LABELS word beside the editor bar and/or role=meter with aria-valuenow/label.

LowNo prefers-reduced-motion support anywhereSAccessibility

The OS reduced-motion signal is unhonored; low impact given the motion is small.

Evidence No prefers-reduced-motion match in web/src; motion at styles.css:225,227,275,286; Vault.tsx:1338.

Fix Add a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block zeroing transitions/animations and the hover lift.

LowNo main landmark or skip link, appbar is a div, and signed-in views have no h1MAccessibility (WCAG 2.4.1/1.3.1)

No main landmark or skip link past the appbar's several tab stops, and an incomplete heading outline in-app.

Evidence Vault.tsx:367,423; ViewHeader.tsx:6 (h2); grep: no main/header/skip/h1 in signed-in views.

Fix Wrap the appbar in <header>, content in <main id=main>, add a visually-hidden skip-to-main link, and promote each view title to h1 (or add an sr-only h1).

LowAdd/edit TOTP field label is developer jargon and contradicts the friendly 'One-time code' detail labelSUX microcopy

The add-login screen uses the most technical wording while the detail view uses friendly wording for the identical field.

Evidence Vault.tsx:1552 (editor label), 1332 (detail label), 1467 (error).

Fix Label it 'One-time code (2FA)' to match the detail view, with a plain hint as secondary text.

Low'break-glass' / 'public address' ops jargon leaks into member-facing 2FA and sign-in copySUX microcopy

'break-glass', 'the public address', 'connect from inside (VPN/LAN)' are unparseable to a household member on an anxiety-prone security screen.

Evidence Settings.tsx:160,167,174,202-205; Welcome.tsx:310,357.

Fix Reframe as 'when you sign in from outside your home network'; drop 'break-glass' from user copy (keep it in comments).

LowDelete confirmation omits that the item is recoverable from Trash for 30 daysSUX microcopy

The soft-delete confirm reads as permanent ('from every device') so users hesitate more than warranted and never learn Trash exists.

Evidence Vault.tsx:976,978,1053,1082.

Fix Add 'You can restore it from Trash for 30 days.' to the confirm.

Low'escrow' jargon persists in the Admin surface while the member flow uses 'admin backstop'SUX microcopy

The Admin table column, download button and status say 'escrow' while the rest of the app (and even other Admin copy) says 'admin backstop'.

Evidence Admin.tsx:130,191,221,776 (raw 'escrow') vs :348,474,527 + Welcome.tsx:650,681 ('admin backstop').

Fix Rename the residual Admin 'escrow' labels to 'admin backstop' / 'Download backstop key' for consistency.

NitEmpty-hoard mark is reduced to a generic rhombus rather than the ᛝ runeSiconography / empty-state

The first-run empty state shows a bare rhombus stranded above dead space. Legitimate polish opportunity but subjective and intentional.

Evidence Sigil.tsx:19-25 (plain diamond, comment admits the reduction); rendered Vault.tsx:539, Health.tsx:118; .empty padding 60px 0 (styles.css:260).

Fix Restore enough ᛝ geometry (doubled/crossed diamond) to read as the rune and tighten the empty-state vertical rhythm.

NitCluttered micro type-scale: seven small sizes 10–14px, several 0.5px apartMtypographic scale

The metadata tier packs seven near-identical sizes (12 vs 12.5, 13 vs 13.5 imperceptible), reading as drift. Low-impact polish.

Evidence styles.css small sizes 10/11/12/12.5/13/13.5/14 across lines 145-357 (tabs at 140 are 14px, not the finder's 13px).

Fix Collapse to ~4 tokenized small steps (11/12/13/14) mapped to roles; drop the .5px variants.

NitCorner-radius set is ad-hoc (2/5/7/9/10/14/16px); card and sheet round differentlySvisual consistency

Seven radii, several nearly identical; card and sheet are conceptually the same object but differ 16 vs 14.

Evidence styles.css:34,118,140,218,240,248,255,264,274,286.

Fix Define a 6/10/14 scale, map card+sheet to the same large step, route 7/9 one-offs to tokens.

NitBrandSigil geometry crosses the full stave rather than branching from one side, so it doesn't read as ᛅSiconography / metaphor

ᛅ (long-branch ár) attaches its branch on one side; the drawn diagonal crosses the stave entirely, reading as a struck-through stave. Deliberate abstraction the finder disputes on authenticity grounds.

Evidence Sigil.tsx:9 (comment) + 13-14 (paths); only rendered on auth/loading cards.

Fix Redraw with the branch terminating at the stave on a single side of x=12; verify against a rendered card.

NitEmpty-vault state has no CTA and ignores the Import first-run pathSstate coverage / first-run

The first-run empty view offers no click target of its own and doesn't surface the Import flow, though the toolbar sits just above it.

Evidence Vault.tsx:538-540 (no CTA); toolbar actions at :493-535 (visible above).

Fix Give the empty state 'Add your first login' and 'Import from another password manager' CTAs mirroring the toolbar.

NitRecovery-phrase reveal has no screenshot-shielding cueStrust-UX / recovery ceremony

The full-plaintext recovery phrase is shown without warning against screenshots, the most common quiet exfiltration of exactly this value.

Evidence Welcome.tsx:726-728 (copy), 735 (userSelect:all).

Fix Add one line: 'Write it down or put it in a password-safe place — don't screenshot it.'

MV3 popup + a content-script autofill overlay in a closed shadow root. Its tokens are a hand-copy of the web CSS.

MediumBrand sigil ships as a raw Runic codepoint (tofu risk), diverging from the web's SVG markMVisual design, brand & iconography

Primary brand mark on every surface renders via textContent of bare U+16C5, falling back to whatever Runic font the OS has (none on stock macOS/many Linux) → tofu on the hero, empty-state, and anti-spoof dropdown/banner header. Two sub-defects: empty-state uses ᛅ not the ᛝ the web reserves (dup of F22); the ⚄ die (U+2684) can render as a color emoji. Overlaps F22 on the empty rune.

Evidence popup.html:10,36; popup.ts:228-229,250,605; content-ui.ts:274,507. dist/popup.js 3×/content.js 2× rune (as ᛅ), 0 createElementNS|viewBox|<svg> in built JS. web/src/ui/Sigil.tsx:10-26 (SVG BrandSigil ᛅ / EmptySigil ᛝ). ⚄ die at popup.html:64 confirmed. Empty-state ᛅ at popup.ts:228 vs web ᛝ.

Fix Port web BrandSigil/EmptySigil inline-SVG (currentColor stroke, CSP-clean) into the extension and swap every textContent ᛅ/ᛝ call site; use ᛝ for the empty state; replace ⚄ with a monochrome SVG.

MediumLight-theme gold interactive text fails WCAG AA (popup + overlay), AM-1 --link not ported, no contrast test covers --goldSAccessibility (WCAG 1.4.3)

Every light-mode gold TEXT affordance — footer web-vault link, saved-site links, Fill/copy/reveal pill labels in list+detail, and the overlay dropdown's action rows — is sub-AA. Untestable today because the test never inspects --gold.

Evidence popup.css:45,222,249,255,305; content-ui.ts:52,109. Ratios 3.74 / 4.05 / 4.05 (all <4.5). web styles.css:26,55,279 --link. contrast.test.ts:52-87 never evaluates --gold. Dark: 8.42/7.73:1 (pass).

Fix Port AM-1: add --link (#d0a94a dark / #7d5e14 light) to popup.css + content-ui.ts token sets; swap .link/.site-link/.fill-btn/.row.action from var(--gold) to var(--link) (leave --gold for gradients/sigil). Extend contrast.test.ts to assert --link clears 4.5:1 on --bg/--bg-raised in both themes.

MediumLight-theme danger text on tinted strips fails AA — .msg.err and the recovery-sign-in must-change stripSAccessibility (WCAG 1.4.3)

The shared #msg error strip (wrong-master-password, clipboard/backend errors) and the security-critical must-change recovery strip render marginally sub-AA in light theme because the semi-transparent danger tint lightens the effective background.

Evidence popup.css:48,145,164-170. Tinted bg = #f0dfd3 (verified). light --danger 4.39:1 (<4.5); dark 4.59:1. F27's '4.20:1' for must-change is actually 4.39:1.

Fix Darken light --danger toward #a5381f (≈5.09:1 on the tinted strip) or drop the tint opacity; add a contrast.test.ts case for --danger against the computed tinted-strip bg in both themes.

MediumIn-page autofill dropdown is mouse-only — rows have no keyboard operability or ARIA rolesMAccessibility (WCAG 2.1.1, 4.1.2)

Keyboard/SR users cannot select a credential from the injected dropdown: rows aren't focusable, aren't announced as options, and respond only to a physical mouse press.

Evidence content-ui.ts:285-296,298-308,329-339; only ARIA is the search aria-label (:349) + toast live regions (:230,:233).

Fix In A1b: role=listbox/option + roving tabindex, arrow/Home/End + Enter/Space activation (move off mousedown while preserving preventDefault), aria-activedescendant; keep the popup as the documented accessible path meanwhile.

MediumSave/update and link-offer banners are neither announced nor focus-managed and auto-dismissMAccessibility (WCAG 4.1.3, 2.4.3)

A save/update offer is silent to SR users and vanishes after 30s (20s for link); focus is never moved in, so a keyboard user must Tab the whole page to reach Save/Not-now. The higher-frequency save path got no live region while the toast path did.

Evidence content-ui.ts:420-464,467-500,446,483; live regions only in showToast (:514-521).

Fix Mirror the banner prompt into the persistent polite region on show (no focus steal, keep timers) as the minimum; consider role=alertdialog+focus for the save offer; pause auto-dismiss while AT focus is inside.

MediumLocked screen is a dead-end for a forgotten master password or not-yet-enrolled memberSUX flows — onboarding / recovery / microcopy

A new invited member who installs the extension first can't proceed and is told nothing; a member who forgets the master password gets a bare wrong-password line with no pointer that recovery needs the recovery phrase — the highest-stakes ZK microcopy is absent.

Evidence popup.html:34-53,102,30-32; errors.ts:39; popup.ts:752-755. No enroll/recover signpost or ZK 'no reset' copy anywhere on the surface.

Fix Add under Unlock a link row 'New here? Enroll in the web vault →' and 'Forgot your master password? Recover with your recovery phrase →', each deep-linking to the web route, reusing the must-change strip's tab-open pattern.

MediumClipboard auto-clear is completely uncommunicated — no countdown, and a late paste fails silentlySTrust-UX: clipboard auto-clear

A genuinely good security feature is never surfaced: the reassurance benefit is forfeited, and a user who pastes after the window finds an empty clipboard and reasonably concludes the copy failed.

Evidence popup.ts:458-463,432,523,676; content.ts:90-98,109-114. No user-facing 'clears in Ns' copy; clearSeconds already returned to the popup.

Fix Fold the window into the existing feedback — 'Copied — clears in 30s' using the clearSeconds the SW returns (generic fallback when unknown); at minimum on the password/card/2FA copies. Zero new plumbing.

MediumNo 'use a different account' / sign-out — switching household members means manually clearing the email fieldSUX flows — account switching

In a shared browser profile a second member finds the first member's email pre-filled with no affordance to switch; locking doesn't reset identity.

Evidence popup.html:101-102; popup.ts:706,126-130; showView(false) at :87-99 clears lists but not #email.

Fix Add a 'Use a different account' link on the locked view that clears lastEmail + the field and focuses email; show the signed-in email somewhere in the unlocked view.

MediumOffline under-communicated on the locked screen; the 'about 6 seconds' KDF bar shows during the pre-KDF network phaseMUX flows — connectivity states / progress affordance

A member off the tailnet gets only a subtle grey dot until they spend an unlock; and the indeterminate 'about 6 seconds' bar is raised for the network phase too, hardcoded rather than derived, over-promising on slow first connections.

Evidence popup.ts:779,687-692; popup.html:49-52; popup.css:104,179-186; background.ts:556-557 (prelogin before KDF).

Fix Render a calm inline locked-view note when the dot is off ('Can't reach the server — check you're on the home network or VPN'); soften the caption to 'this can take a few seconds' (escalate after ~10s); consider showing the bar only once the KDF actually starts.

MediumPopup has no outer scroll containment — stacked banners or a many-URI detail can push Lock and 'Fill this page' below Chrome's 600px capMResponsive/platform-fit: popup 360px overflow

In a recovery session (must-change strip) plus an error, or a login with a dozen+ URIs, the footer Lock and the detail's primary Fill action get pushed below the fold — the manual-lock and primary-fill affordances should never be scrolled away, least of all in the heightened-trust recovery moment.

Evidence popup.css:191,235,299-302; popup.ts:385-401,407-413. Body/detail have no max-height; footer not sticky.

Fix Give .detail a max-height + internal overflow-y on the URI list (mirror .list); make .pop-foot sticky (position:sticky;bottom:0) or cap the body and scroll the middle. Re-check the budget with update-banner + must-change + #msg all present.

LowType scale is ad-hoc: ~15 font sizes in a 360px popup, several 0.5px apartMVisual design, brand & iconography

The small end is a cluster of near-imperceptible steps that flatten hierarchy rather than reinforce it. Display sizes (18/21/38) are used well.

Evidence popup.css distinct font-sizes (verified by grep): 10,10.5,11,11.5,12,12.5,13,13.5,14,15,18,20,21,30,38 = 15 steps; 12.5px used 10×, 11.5px 6×.

Fix Collapse to a small named ramp (11/12.5/13.5/15/18/21/38) and assign each element role a single scale token.

Low--gold-bright inverts luminance between themes, flipping the primary-button gradient directionSVisual design, brand & iconography

The token pair swaps luminance order across themes, so any rule treating gold-bright as 'the lighter gold' reads inverted in light mode; most visible on the primary button's raking-light direction.

Evidence popup.css:21-22 (dark), 45-46 (light); gradient at popup.css:132, content-ui.ts:149. Web keeps --link distinct precisely to avoid flattening this gradient (styles.css:53-55).

Fix Rename to intent (--gold-emphasis) or invert the light-theme gradient stop order so the button reads lit-from-above in both themes; document the rule.

Lowcontent-ui --anv-ink-faint is a dead, drifted token in a third untested copy of the paletteSAccessibility (WCAG 1.4.3)

Harmless today (unused), but the overlay is an independent third hand-maintained token copy that no test covers; the moment any overlay rule uses var(--anv-ink-faint) it ships sub-AA text with nothing to catch it.

Evidence content-ui.ts:30,51 (defined, never referenced). popup.css:20,44 raised to #8d8370/#786c50. Ratios 3.06/3.33/3.02:1. contrast.test.ts parses only ../popup.css.

Fix Delete the dead lines (or sync to #8d8370/#786c50); collapse the three token copies to one shared source, or add a build-time cross-copy assertion; optionally extend the contrast test to parse UI_CSS.

LowNo custom focus-visible indicator on buttons/rows — keyboard focus relies on the UA default ringSAccessibility (WCAG 2.4.7, 2.4.11)

Every interactive control except text inputs falls back to the UA outline, which on #14120e charcoal can be low-contrast while tabbing rows/copy buttons — the keyboard user's primary operation path.

Evidence popup.css:123 (input only), :230 (.acts opacity via :focus-within); no other focus rule.

Fix Add a shared :focus-visible { outline:2px solid var(--gold); outline-offset:2px } (or the existing gold box-shadow ring) to the interactive controls.

LowLogin row is role=button containing nested real buttons — invalid ARIA nesting and ambiguous nameMAccessibility (WCAG 4.1.2)

SR announces a verbose/confusing name for the row and the button-in-button nesting is technically non-conformant, though the nested buttons remain separately focusable.

Evidence popup.ts:241-280,265-269,491; actBtn creates <button> (:417-428).

Fix In A1b restructure so the row is a non-button container with one named primary control (or make the row the only button and host copy/TOTP in the detail view).

LowReveal toggle's accessible name doesn't track show/hide stateSTrust-UX: secret reveal/hide affordance

On a masked-by-default surface, the one control that exposes a password doesn't report its state to AT — a SR user gets no signal the secret is now visible on screen.

Evidence popup.ts:321-336,355,422; no aria-label/aria-pressed update in toggleReveal.

Fix Update aria-label alongside textContent (Reveal↔Hide password) and add aria-pressed to expose state programmatically.

LowRow/card copy buttons show cryptic abbreviations as visible labels (user/pass/num/exp/cvv)SMicrocopy — labels / clarity

The visible pill text a non-technical member reads is developer shorthand; cvv/exp are opaque, clashing with the warm-household voice and the detail view's clear verbs.

Evidence popup.ts:267-268,619-621,420 (textContent=label); detail verbs at :346,356.

Fix Use glyph icons or the detail view's verbs; at minimum user→name, pass→copy, num→number, exp→expiry, cvv→code.

LowEmpty-hoard state uses the brand sigil ᛅ (not ᛝ) and offers no next stepSMicrocopy / brand — empty states

Reuses the brand rune where the system reserves ᛝ, and the message is terminal though the extension can't add items so it most needs a pointer.

Evidence popup.ts:228,201; web Vault.tsx:539 EmptySigil; Sigil.tsx:20 (ᛝ).

Fix Use ᛝ and add a next step: 'The hoard is empty — sign into a site to save your first login, or add one in the web vault.'

LowGenerator is bare — no length/charset controls, no strength meter, and the in-page fill has no previewMUX flows — generator

A member on a format-constrained site can only blind-regenerate; the in-page strong-password action commits a value they never see.

Evidence popup.html:67-74; popup.ts:658-683; content-ui.ts:337; content.ts:173-187.

Fix Add a collapsible length control + symbols/numbers toggle; echo the generated value into a revealable field or brief confirmation for the in-page path.

LowSelf-update banner 'Download & reload' is opaque for the unpacked household installSMicrocopy — update flow

Two words compress a multi-step manual dance a non-technical member can't act on; since unpacked extensions can't self-install, this banner is the whole update UX.

Evidence popup.ts:144,151; INSTALL.txt:34-38 (unzip-over + reload-icon + reload-tabs).

Fix Reword to 'Get the update' linking to the short install/update steps rather than implying one-click reload.

LowA TOTP chip hides itself permanently on a transient poll failure — a 2FA code can silently vanish mid-sessionSTrust-UX: 2FA surfacing honesty

A one-time code that quietly disappears from its row erodes trust and forces re-opening the item; a per-chip failure should be recoverable, not terminal.

Evidence popup.ts:567-569 (chip.hidden=true, never reset); status guard at :548.

Fix On a transient per-chip failure keep the chip visible with a neutral placeholder and let the next successful tick repopulate; hide only when status confirms the item no longer carries a code.

NitCorner radii use three arbitrary values (7/8/10px) for same-scale elementsSVisual design, brand & iconography

Small controls of similar weight carry 7/8/10px with no system; costs a little of the crafted feel.

Evidence popup.css:29,213,223,250; content-ui.ts:39,123,150,161.

Fix Define --radius 10 (containers) + --radius-sm 7 (pills/small controls); replace loose 8px/7px literals; align content-ui.

NitCoin-monogram letter can wash out on the lit quadrant of its own gradientSVisual design, brand & iconography

First-letter monogram is bright gold over a warm-gold highlight, so contrast against the top-left lit quadrant drops to ~3:1 and the letter partly dissolves into the coin.

Evidence popup.css:212-217,27-28,51-52. Ratios 2.96 / 9.61 / 3.30:1 (verified).

Fix Deepen --glyph-hi below the monogram's tone, or ink the monogram darker (e.g. --btn-ink) so it reads struck-into the coin.

NitPopup has no heading structure — section labels and brand are non-heading spans/divsSAccessibility (WCAG 1.3.1, 2.4.6)

SR users can't use heading navigation between the site list, all-logins, and cards; no programmatic top-level heading. Minor since landmarks exist.

Evidence popup.html:10,75,77,86; popup.ts:580-581. No heading roles anywhere.

Fix Give .section-label titles role=heading aria-level=2 (or <h2>) and add a visually-hidden <h1>andvari</h1>.

NitCold-start popup shows a blank window with no loading affordance until the service worker answersSState coverage — loadingplausible

During a cold SW wake the branded header + footer render but the middle (locked form) is empty with no spinner. The finding's 'blank / no brand' framing is inaccurate.

Evidence popup.html:9-15 header (not hidden), :99-108 footer (not hidden), :34/55/83 sections hidden; popup.ts:102-109,776.

Fix Optionally show a lightweight '…' or sigil placeholder in the content area until status resolves; the header/brand already paints from static HTML.

NitCopy drift between the two 'no match' empty lines in a codebase that pins copy verbatimSMicrocopy — consistency

The dropdown and popup phrase the same empty state two ways — exactly the cross-surface drift the error canon exists to prevent.

Evidence content-ui.ts:335; popup.ts:191; errors.ts:1-8 (verbatim-copy canon).

Fix Pick one sentence ('No login saved for {host}') and use it in both surfaces.

Kotlin + Compose + Material3 — the surface most household members use daily. No @Preview; hardest to visually iterate.

Native surface — no headless render path (no @Preview); audited from source. Contrast ratios computed from declared token hexes.

HighTheme.kt overrides only the legacy base color roles — M3 1.3.1 paints Cards, dialogs, the FAB and error/tertiary surfaces from un-overridden container rolesMVisual design, brand & iconography

Because only the pre-Material-You base roles are set, the tonal container roles M3 1.3 actually paints surfaces with stay at the Material baseline: routine cards render cool-gray, the most prominent action (the Add FAB) renders lavender-purple, the re-seal card mauve. The treasury identity is largely absent on-device — the 'generic Material template' look the brand is trying to avoid.

Evidence Theme.kt:25-49 (dark sets 11 roles, none of the container ramp); MainActivity.kt:818/822 FAB has no colors→primaryContainer; :646 ReSealCard tertiaryContainer; :492 tertiary. Card default token in material3 1.3.1 = surfaceContainerHighest (baseline #36343B). Verified warm-surface vs baseline-card = 1.40:1.

Fix Complete both schemes: set primaryContainer/onPrimaryContainer, tertiary/tertiaryContainer, errorContainer/onErrorContainer, and the surfaceContainerLow…Highest ramp to warm treasury tones; give the FAB a gold container. Verify Card/AlertDialog paint the warm surface, not #36343B.

HighIn-app brand marks render the raw runic codepoints ᛅ/ᛝ as Text — the exact tofu case the SVG BrandSigil was built to avoidMVisual design, brand & iconography

The rune is the brand's primary mark on first-run Welcome, every Unlock, the autofill overlay, and the empty vault. Tofu (or a mismatched font glyph) at exactly the trust-critical moments reads as broken and contradicts the team's own reason for shipping the mark as SVG on web.

Evidence MainActivity.kt:217,351,849; AutofillUnlockActivity.kt:296; SaveConfirmActivity.kt:485 all Text("ᛅ"/"ᛝ"). No bundled font. web/src/ui/Sigil.tsx:3 documents the tofu risk; :10-16 ships BrandSigil as SVG.

Fix Render the BrandSigil geometry in Compose (ImageVector/vector-drawable or a small Canvas), reusing the launcher path so in-app/launcher/web are coherent; drive it with colorScheme.primary. Same for the empty-state ᛝ.

HighError-banner text on errorContainer fails WCAG AA (2.56:1 dark) — the app's primary error surfaceSAccessibility (WCAG 1.4.3)

The ErrorBar is the app's main channel for wrong-password/import/unlock failures and the security-critical lifecycle notices, rendering at ~2.56:1 in the default dark theme.

Evidence Theme.kt:23,36; MainActivity.kt:390/251/332 error-on-errorContainer = 2.56:1 (verified); SharingScreen.kt:104/109. Body lines use onErrorContainer (pass). Contradicts docs/design/2026-07-11-accessibility-a1.md:181.

Fix Paint text/labels sitting on errorContainer with onErrorContainer instead of .error (ErrorBar :390, MustChange/UnopenableVault titles, LifecycleNotices warn body, dismiss label). Leave error-toned text on plain surface as-is.

HighCopying a secret gives zero in-app feedback and never reveals the clipboard auto-clear countdown (merges the duplicate 'auto-clear never communicated' finding)SUX flows / Password-manager trust-UX (clipboard auto-clear)

Copying a secret — the core daily action — produces no in-app confirmation, and the app auto-wipes the clipboard after the policy window (default 30s) without ever telling the user, turning a safety feature into 'why did my paste fail?'. On API 29-32 there is no OS toast either.

Evidence MainActivity.kt:2240-2265 (clear scheduled silently); call sites :2143/2158/1506/608/2036; no Toast/Snackbar (grep NONE); AutofillStatusScreen.kt:308 proves feedback is feasible; clipClear at :1183 never shown. minSdk29 (build.gradle.kts:25).

Fix On every secret copy set ui.notice (the existing NoticeBar/liveRegion path) to e.g. 'Copied — clears in {N}s', reusing maxOf(1, clipboardClearSeconds) at :1183, or add a transient 'Copied' button state with a countdown.

MediumSerif display is mapped to only two type roles, so heading hierarchy splits into two unrelated systemsMVisual design, brand & iconography

Anything a designer treats as a heading that lands on titleMedium/titleSmall/headlineSmall silently becomes sans, so some card headers are large ornate serif and adjacent ones plain sans; the brand serif voice doesn't carry to sub-headings.

Evidence Theme.kt:52-55; titleLarge card headers at MainActivity 1606/1624/1636/1658/1675, SharingScreen 221/532/579; sans headers at MainActivity 587/656, SharingScreen 462.

Fix Decide the serif's job and apply it consistently: extend serif to the title roles used as headers, or demote repeated card-section labels off titleLarge onto one sans label role; normalize screen titles to one serif role. Consider dialing 22sp card headers down.

MediumAutofill overlay error messages are silent to TalkBack (no live region)SAccessibility (WCAG 4.1.3)

In the locked autofill path a blind user who mistypes the master password gets no spoken feedback — the error appears but isn't announced.

Evidence AutofillUnlockActivity.kt:309-312; SaveConfirmActivity.kt:415-418/457-460/494 — error Text, no liveRegion. Contrast with MainActivity.kt:388/2024.

Fix Add Modifier.semantics { liveRegion = LiveRegionMode.Assertive } to the error Text in each of the four autofill cards, mirroring ErrorBar.

MediumVault search field has no programmatic label (placeholder-only)SAccessibility (WCAG 1.3.1 / 4.1.2)

TalkBack announces this as a bare 'edit box' with no purpose and no accessible name once the user starts typing.

Evidence MainActivity.kt:840 — no label; leadingIcon contentDescription=null.

Fix Add label={Text("Search vault")} (parity with desktop Ui.kt:428), or Modifier.semantics{ contentDescription = "Search vault" } if the floating label is unwanted.

MediumCopy buttons all announce just 'Copy' — indistinguishable across item-detail rowsSAccessibility (WCAG 4.1.2)

A screen-reader user navigating control-by-control lands on buttons all named 'Copy' — copying the username instead of the password (or CVV vs PAN) has real consequence.

Evidence MainActivity.kt:2143 (CopyRow), :2158 (SecretCopyRow), :2036 (SelectableCopyRow); named counter-example at :1507.

Fix Add Modifier.semantics { contentDescription = "Copy $label" } to the TextButton in CopyRow, SecretCopyRow and SelectableCopyRow (label already in scope), matching TotpRow.

MediumEnroll recovery ceremony assumes the invitee holds the printed sheet and overloads 'recovery' four waysMUX flows, microcopy, interaction & states

An invited member usually doesn't hold the household's printed sheet (the admin does) yet can't complete enrollment without the 16 chars, with no in-app guidance; they must also separate the org 'recovery key/fingerprint/sheet' from their own 'recovery phrase' — two secrets both branded 'recovery'.

Evidence MainActivity.kt:509-524,552-556 (gated on printed-sheet 16 chars + checkbox, no fallback); four 'recovery' terms across :509-543 vs RecoverySetupScreen :587-616.

Fix Add a hint under the field ('Don't have the sheet? Ask whoever invited you to read you the first 16 characters'); rename so the two concepts don't collide (e.g. keep 'recovery phrase' for the member secret; call the org check a 'household verification code').

MediumManual 'sync' and background sync give no progress or success feedback; no pull-to-refreshMUX flows, microcopy, interaction & states

Tapping sync appears inert and the 5-minute background sync is invisible, so 'did my new password reach my other devices?' is unanswerable; the missing pull-to-refresh also breaks a near-universal Android expectation.

Evidence MainActivity.kt:804 sync icon→vm.refresh(); AndvariViewModel.kt:1294-1320 sets ui.syncing (rendered nowhere); MainActivity.kt:839 plain scroll, no PullRefresh (grep NONE).

Fix Surface ui.syncing as a top-bar indicator/linear progress and set a brief ui.notice ('Synced') on manual refresh; consider wrapping the list in pull-to-refresh mapped to vm.refresh().

MediumSign-in and other unmapped errors surface raw exception textSUX flows, microcopy, interaction & states

Unlike unlock/autofill (which handle offline gracefully), first-time sign-in with no connectivity shows a raw transport string on the very first screen.

Evidence AndvariViewModel.kt:639-656 (no IOException mapping), :751 signIn catch → fail()→friendlyError; offline notice only at :464/:811; MainActivity.kt:423.

Fix In friendlyError, map IOException/connectivity to a human line and give unmapped ApiExceptions a generic fallback so the Welcome ErrorBar never shows transport-layer text.

MediumThe 'Sharing' screen cannot actually share — no add-member or role control, and no signpost to the webSUX flows, microcopy, interaction & states

The primary mental model of 'Sharing' (give my spouse access) is impossible from Android and the screen never says so.

Evidence SharingScreen.kt:149-150 comment; :341-355 owner panel; :270-281 member panel; :222-225 vault card copy — no add-member action or web signpost.

Fix Add an honest line ('To add people or change who can edit, open andvari in your web browser.') and ideally a disabled-with-explanation 'Add member' entry so the capability is discoverable.

MediumVault TopAppBar crams 6 action icons with no overflow menu — violates M3 and risks clipping the titleMResponsive/platform-fit (M3 convention, small screens, text scaling)

Six persistent icons crowd the bar, can clip the wordmark on narrow/large-font devices, and bury the manual Lock (the control that must be easy to hit) as the 6th icon.

Evidence MainActivity.kt:801-809 six IconButtons; title titleLarge 22sp serif at :800; AndroidManifest fontScale configChanges.

Fix Keep sync + lock (and optionally import) top-level; move sharing/trash/settings into a three-dot overflow menu (which also gives them text labels).

LowThe 'coin monogram' is a bare gold serif initial with no coin/disc, undersized, and rendered inconsistently between the two vault listsSVisual design, brand & iconography

The 'first-letter coin monogram' ships as a naked gold initial, small for its slot, and positioned/sized differently in the vault list vs the sharing list, so the same concept looks like two components.

Evidence MainActivity.kt:1082-1084 (Box 38.dp, no shape/bg, default ~14sp); SharingScreen.kt:252 (inline, no Box). No disc/ring anywhere.

Fix Extract one shared Monogram composable (filled/outlined disc, fixed ~36-40dp, explicit type size) and use it in both VaultRow and VaultListRow.

LowLight-theme primary buttons put white text on the gold-brown primary (#9A7420) at 4.29:1 — below AASVisual design, brand & iconography

In light mode the main call-to-action text is marginally under the accessibility floor and reads slightly muddy.

Evidence Theme.kt:40-41 primary=#9A7420, onPrimary=White → 4.29:1 (verified). Filled Button label = labelLarge 14sp.

Fix Darken light primary (e.g. ~#8A6412) so white clears 4.5:1. Note: switching to dark-on-gold does NOT fix it (#1A1509 on #9A7420 = 4.24:1) — the gold itself must darken.

LowWordmark is rendered single-color, dropping the specified two-tone gold 'and' + ink 'vari'SVisual design, brand & iconography

The two-tone treatment is what makes the lockup read as 'andvari' rather than generic lowercase text; single-color loses the signature.

Evidence MainActivity.kt:352,800 single-color; web/src/ui/styles.css:104 + Welcome.tsx:203 render gold 'and' + base 'vari'.

Fix Use an AnnotatedString with gold ('and') + onSurface ('vari') spans in Sigil() (and optionally the top-bar title), matching the web wordmark.

LowLight-theme gold primary drops below 4.5:1 for normal-size textMAccessibility (WCAG 1.4.3)

Only the non-default light theme and small texts are affected, but the trusted-browser confirmation and item avatar initials are genuine normal-size text below AA.

Evidence Theme.kt:40 primary=#9A7420 → 3.74/4.05:1 (verified); AutofillStatusScreen.kt:179; MainActivity.kt:1083; SharingScreen.kt:252.

Fix Darken light primary toward #7d5e14 (5.26:1) or color these specific small texts with secondary (#7D5E14 = 5.68:1, verified) rather than primary.

LowReduced-motion is not honored anywhere (indefinite spinners/progress run regardless)MAccessibility (reduced-motion)

Users who enable 'Remove animations' still get continuously spinning indicators; low because the motion is small and mostly transient.

Evidence No reduced-motion read anywhere (grep NONE); animations at MainActivity.kt:2200/1490-1513/998, SharingScreen.kt:298. Deferred in a1.md:499-500.

Fix When animation-off is detected, swap indefinite spinners for a static/determinate affordance. Track as the A2 candidate the design already flagged.

LowThe multi-second Argon2id unlock shows only a bare button spinner; the 'unsealing' voice is absent everywhereSUX flows, microcopy, interaction & states

During the several-second Argon2id derivation the only feedback is a tiny button spinner with no reassuring copy, and the Loading screen has no text — a missed brand-voice moment and a cross-surface inconsistency.

Evidence MainActivity.kt:718/2198-2202 spinner-only; :217 Loading rune no text; AutofillUnlockActivity.kt:319; no 'unseal' copy (grep NONE).

Fix Add a short 'Unsealing…' label by the unlock spinner and a line on Screen.Loading in the established household voice.

LowAutofill unlock overlay password field has no show/hide toggleSUX flows, microcopy, interaction & states

When autofill prompts for the master password the user can't reveal what they typed; on a mistype ('Wrong master password.') there's no way to verify the entry — more friction than the main Unlock screen.

Evidence AutofillUnlockActivity.kt:300-308 (no reveal) vs MainActivity.kt:2129-2134 SecretField.

Fix Reuse the SecretField reveal idiom (trailing eye toggle) in UnlockCard.

LowNo breach/HIBP surfacing anywhere on Android despite the web Health viewLPassword-manager trust-UX (breach/HIBP surfacing)

A mobile-primary household user never learns on their phone that a saved password is breached/weak/reused — a core trust feature present on web is silently missing on the surface most people use daily.

Evidence app-android grep NONE for breach/hibp/health; web/src/crypto/hibp.ts present; Settings cards MainActivity.kt:1590-1728 have no health card.

Fix Add a read-only Password Health card using the same k-anonymity HIBP range check the web uses; if deferring, note in-app that health checks live on the web client.

LowRecovery-phrase type-back confirmation can be satisfied by copy→paste, weakening the shown-once ceremonyMPassword-manager trust-UX (recovery-phrase ceremony)

The type-back exists to prove the user recorded the phrase offline, but copy→paste bypasses it — the exact silent-total-loss the ceremony guards against; conversely hand-typing a long base64url string is error-prone.

Evidence MainActivity.kt:604 SelectionContainer, :608 'Copy phrase', :616 pasteable Field, :627 confirm gate.
