Fix Drop paste into the confirm field (or clear/disable if the pasted text equals the just-copied phrase), or reframe 'Copy phrase' as an explicit save action; add a 'This is the only time you'll see it' cue near the reveal.

LowCard PAN and TOTP secret are shown in cleartext by default in the editor while CVV and password are maskedSPassword-manager trust-UX (secret reveal/hide defaults)

The full PAN and TOTP shared secret are as sensitive as CVV/password yet are the only sensitive fields displayed openly during entry — the inconsistency reads as an oversight and exposes the PAN to shoulder-surfing.

Evidence MainActivity.kt:1382-1385 PAN plain, :1373 TOTP plain, :1366 password SecretField, :1403 CVV SecretField; :1194 detail masks PAN.

Fix Give the card-number and TOTP editor fields the same SecretField masked-with-reveal treatment (reveal off by default), keeping mono + autocorrect-off.

NitAutofill dropdown row is hardcoded dark charcoal regardless of the system/browser themeSVisual design, brand & iconography

A permanently-dark suggestion row can look out of place over a light form — worth a conscious call, but on-brand and legible.

Evidence autofill_dataset.xml:10,33,44 hardcoded literals; header comment documents the RemoteViews constraint.

Fix Accept as-is, or provide a res/layout-night vs res/layout pair so the ground tone follows the device theme while keeping gold as the accent.

NitLauncher monochrome (themed-icon) layer reuses the hairline-stroked foreground, so the themed icon reads faintSVisual design, brand & iconography

In themed-icon mode the sigil becomes two hairlines with a lot of empty space, weak next to peers that fill their keyline.

Evidence ic_launcher.xml:5 monochrome→foreground; ic_launcher_foreground.xml:18-26 strokeWidth 1.8 × scale 2.4.

Fix Ship a bolder/optionally filled dedicated monochrome drawable so the themed icon holds its weight.

Nit'Enroll' tab and 'Invite token' label read clinically for a household memberSUX flows, microcopy, interaction & states

'Enroll' and 'Invite token' are mildly institutional/technical for a warm household product.

Evidence MainActivity.kt:420 tab 'Enroll'; :480 field 'Invite token'.

Fix Consider 'Set up'/'Join household' for the tab and 'Invite code' for the field.

Compose Multiplatform (MSI/Deb), a 480×720 single window. A fourth hand-copy of the palette; shares Android’s Compose gaps.

Native surface — no headless render path (no @Preview); audited from source. Contrast ratios computed from declared token hexes.

HighZero live regions in the entire desktop client — errors/lock reason/import status silent to a screen readerMAccessibility (WCAG) 4.1.3

A blind user typing a wrong master password hears nothing — no failure, no idle-lock notice. The design's cross-client P2 principle was applied everywhere but desktop.

Evidence 0 liveRegion in app-desktop (grep). ErrorBar/NoticeBar render conditionally with no liveRegion; affects Unlock error (419), lock reason (415), import errors/results (606-701), editor save error (1840), mustChange banner (95-103). Android bars have it.

Fix Add liveRegion=Assertive to ErrorBar, Polite to NoticeBar; drive lockReason/import progress from one always-composed visually-hidden announcer node.

MediumBrand marks ᛅ/ᛝ are raw Runic codepoints — no SVG geometry ported from webMVisual design, brand & iconographyplausible

Brand sigil (loading, welcome/unlock hero, empty-vault) rests on two raw Runic glyphs in the platform logical serif. On a minimal Linux Deb target with no Runic-covering font, Skia paints missing-glyph boxes — the failure web already solved with geometry and never ported. Windows likely renders via DirectWrite fallback.

Evidence Ui.kt:119,138,533 = Text("ᛅ"/"ᛝ", headlineMedium); headlineMedium→Serif (Main.kt:48). 0 Canvas/vector in app-desktop (grep). web Sigil.tsx renders the same two glyphs as SVG paths, comment says raw runic 'render as tofu wherever no installed font covers the Runic block'. Targets Msi+Deb (build.gradle.kts:27).

Fix Port BrandSigil/EmptySigil to a Compose Canvas/vector tracing the same two paths (stroke=colorScheme.primary) and replace the three Text call sites, so the mark is font-independent on both Msi and Deb.

MediumHalf the Material color roles are un-themed — baseline purple/pink bleeds into the gold treasurySVisual design, brand & iconography

The highest-attention surfaces (update banner, warnings, break-glass re-seal) render in M3 default lavender/pink/mauve rather than warm gold/bronze — off-brand hue, though contrast passes. Light captions also fall to the cool #49454F baseline vs dark's warm #A79C85.

Evidence Main.kt DarkColors/LightColors set only primary/onPrimary/secondary/background/onBackground/surface/onSurface/surfaceVariant/onSurfaceVariant/outline/error. Unset roles used at Ui.kt:82 (secondaryContainer), 293/640/1766/1779 (tertiary), 182 (tertiaryContainer), 96/150 (errorContainer). Light omits onSurfaceVariant.

Fix Add warm-tuned tertiary/tertiaryContainer, secondaryContainer, errorContainer, outlineVariant to both schemes and set a warm light onSurfaceVariant. ~8 hex additions.

MediumResting text-field borders near-invisible on charcoal (outline #38311F ≈ 1.3:1)SVisual design, brand & iconography

At rest the input border is essentially invisible until focus paints it gold; the auth screens read as floating labels with no visible boxes.

Evidence Main.kt:40 outline=#38311F. Verified 1.33:1 vs #1E1B15, 1.45:1 vs #14120E. Unfocused border for Field/Secret/ServerField (Ui.kt:2324,2334,2366).

Fix Lighten dark outline enough to clear 3:1 vs #1E1B15 — note the finder's #5A4E30 only reaches 2.10:1, so go lighter/warmer (e.g. toward ~#7A6B45); also give Divider a distinct warm outlineVariant.

MediumLight-theme gold primary #9A7420 fails WCAG AA as small text (badges/URL) — MERGEDSAccessibility (WCAG) 1.4.3

In light mode the gold shared-vault badge (meaningful, non-decorative small text) renders below AA; the a11y pass that fixed the identical web token never touched desktop's palette.

Evidence Main.kt:43 primary=#9A7420 → 3.74:1/4.05:1 (verified). Used labelSmall badges Ui.kt:779,1533; URL :74. secondary already #7D5E14 (5.26/5.68:1). Dark primary #D0A94A fine (7.7-8.4:1, verified).

Fix Set light primary (or a separate badge/link token) to #7D5E14 — value already in secondary; re-check primary-button onPrimary after.

MediumTotpRow copy button's accessible name IS the live TOTP secret; countdown churns the a11y treeSAccessibility (WCAG) 1.1.1, 4.1.2

A SR user hears the secret digits read as the button label and per-second countdown churn instead of a usable 'copy one-time code'.

Evidence Ui.kt:1888 button child = code; :1889 '${remaining}s' no clearAndSetSemantics; label 'One-time code' is a sibling (1886). Android idiom at MainActivity.kt:1507/1511.

Fix contentDescription="Copy one-time code" on the button; wrap the countdown in clearAndSetSemantics{}.

MediumCopy and reveal controls are undifferentiated — no field association in the accessible nameSAccessibility (WCAG) 4.1.2

On a multi-secret detail the copy/reveal buttons are indistinguishable by name; the label exists but isn't part of the control's accessible name.

Evidence Ui.kt:2346 label sibling Text; :2349 generic reveal name; :2350 bare 'Copy'. Repeated for every secret row.

Fix Thread the label: contentDescription="Copy $label" and "Show/Hide $label", or mergeDescendants on the row.

MediumInline validation errors are orphaned sibling Texts — fields never expose isError/supportingTextMAccessibility (WCAG) 1.3.1, 3.3.1, 4.1.2

A SR user focusing a field in error is never told; sighted users also lose Material3's red error outline.

Evidence Ui.kt:2315-2339 Field/Secret set no isError/supportingText; validation emitted as siblings at 290/298/312/1753/1766/1779/2130.

Fix Extend Field/Secret with optional error:String? → set isError=true + supportingText so M3 associates and paints the error state.

MediumUpgradeRequired is a total dead-end with an uncopyable URL — no sign-out escape (Android has one) — MERGEDSInteraction & states / reversibility

On a 426 (or wrong-server pin) the desktop app becomes a screen the user can't leave — no sign-out/change-server/retry, and the download URL can't even be copied. Also removes lock/session controls mid-session. Android already ports an escape.

Evidence Ui.kt:68-77 (no button, return, plain uncopyable URL). Android sign-out escape at MainActivity.kt:380 (+ contrast comment 357-361). Triggered via DesktopState.kt:1744-1748.

Fix Add a 'Sign out / change server' OutlinedButton (signOut()/lock() already exist) and wrap the URL in SelectionContainer or open it via Desktop.browse.

MediumGenerate silently overwrites an existing password with no confirm — web guards with 'Replace?'SInteraction & states / reversibility

Editing a login, one stray Generate click silently replaces the live (masked) credential; Save then propagates the loss everywhere.

Evidence Ui.kt:1747 direct assign; :1719 var password = initial.login?.password. Web guard Vault.tsx:1403/1543.

Fix Mirror web F78: if the password field is non-empty, first Generate arms 'Replace?', a second confirms.

MediumEnroll fingerprint ceremony implies the invitee owns 'your printed recovery sheet', with no upfront 'ask your admin'SOnboarding clarity / microcopyplausible

A fresh invitee has no recovery sheet of their own; the possessive copy and the lack of an always-visible 'don't have this? ask whoever set up your household' can strand onboarding behind a disabled checkbox.

Evidence Ui.kt:309-313 copy + mismatch-only guidance; :320-321 checkbox enabled=shortOk hard-blocks enroll; the enrollee's own phrase is shown only AFTER enroll (RecoverySetup 369).

Fix Reword to name the source ('the household recovery fingerprint — ask whoever set up your household') and add an always-visible helper before any entry.

MediumRecovery sign-in (mustChangePassword) can't be resolved in the desktop app; the URL isn't clickableMState coverage / flow completeness

After an admin recovery, the desktop app hoists a permanent banner sending the mandated password change to a browser and can't even make the URL actionable; native self-recovery is deferred.

Evidence Ui.kt:95-103 banner with embedded non-clickable baseUrl; RecoverySetup TODO 352-354 (self-recovery deferred).

Fix Short term: open the baseUrl via Desktop.browse or at least SelectionContainer it; track native change-master-password + self-recovery as the real fix.

MediumCopying a secret gives zero UI feedback — clipboard auto-clear is invisible (CANONICAL copy-feedback)MPassword-manager trust-UX (clipboard auto-clear communication)

Copy — the core action — produces no acknowledgement for anyone, and the auto-clear window is unsignalled, so a late paste silently fails and the security feature is undiscoverable. Strictly worse than web.

Evidence Platform.kt:95-106 no signal; Ui.kt:2350/1888/2282/387 no feedback; 0 Snackbar/Toast (grep); copiedNote is bulk-copy only (DesktopState.kt:300). Web Copied✓ verified.

Fix Add a transient 'Copied — clears in {n}s' (label swap or Snackbar host) and announce it via the P2 polite announcer; clearSeconds is already in scope at every call site.

MediumLock posture is weak: manual Lock only on the Vault screen, and auto-lock is off when policy autoLockSeconds is 0MPassword-manager trust-UX (lock-state clarity)

On a shared household desktop with no org autoLockSeconds the vault never auto-locks, and while deep in any sub-screen there's neither an auto-lock guarantee nor a reachable manual Lock.

Evidence Ui.kt:462 sole lock; other screens back-only; DesktopState.kt:965-966 window<=0 return; DesktopSession.kt:41 default 0; Main.kt:73 no lock hotkey.

Fix Expose Lock from every signed-in screen's chrome (or Ctrl/Cmd+L via the existing onPreviewKeyEvent), and ship a non-zero client-side auto-lock floor when policy is 0.

MediumA server-revoked/expired session surfaces a raw error and strands the user on a dead Unlock screenMPassword-manager trust-UX (session/revocation honesty)

When a device is disabled or the token expires, the app shows a raw server string and a retry self-reports 'no session' — no honest 'your session ended, sign in again' and no signposted next step.

Evidence DesktopState.kt:1720-1737 (no 401 map), :444-446 (401 wipes store), :436 ('no session'), :135-137 comment, :1000 raw bg error.

Fix Map 401 in friendlyError to honest copy and route the 401-wipe to Welcome (or set lockReason) instead of a dead Unlock screen.

MediumThe 480×720 window has no minimum size — narrowing collapses the 6-icon toolbarSResponsive/platform-fit (window resize)

Dragging the window narrow squeezes the title to zero then clips/overlaps the toolbar icons, with no floor and no tray to recover from a broken narrow state.

Evidence Main.kt:69 no minimumSize (grep=0); Ui.kt:457-462 six icons + weighted title in one Row.

Fix Set window.minimumSize (e.g. 420×560) via the AWT handle in Main.kt's existing DisposableEffect.

LowCoin monogram is an unstyled floating initial — smaller than the title, ragged left edgeMVisual design, brand & iconography

No coin/disc affordance and a variable-width initial means the treasury monogram doesn't land and the vault list left edge is ragged.

Evidence Ui.kt:762 (vault Row) & :1099 (VaultListRow): bare gold serif capital, no style, only padding(end=12dp); name is bodyLarge 16sp (:764).

Fix A fixed ~36dp circular tinted Box with the serif initial centered and sized up (titleMedium+), applied at both call sites.

LowFirst-run recovery screen title drops the serif display face and changes sizeSVisual design, brand & iconography

The un-skippable recovery-phrase gate is the single screen whose title breaks the serif convention and is a different size.

Evidence Ui.kt:369 headlineSmall (unmapped→sans 24sp) vs the headlineMedium-serif used by every other screen title.

Fix Change Ui.kt:369 to headlineMedium (already serif).

LowNo app/launcher icon — MSI and Deb ship the stock Compose placeholderMVisual design, brand & iconography

Installer, Start-menu shortcut, taskbar, and .deb launcher all show the generic Compose logo.

Evidence build.gradle.kts:38-44 windows{} has no iconFile, no linux{} block; only default-icon-{windows.ico,linux.png,mac.icns} exist.

Fix Generate an .ico + .png of the ᛅ mark and wire iconFile.set(...) into windows{} and a new linux{} block.

LowSharing 'recently deleted' toggle signals expanded state by icon color aloneSAccessibility (WCAG) 1.4.1, 4.1.2

SR/color-blind users can't tell whether the disclosure is open; state is conveyed purely by gold-vs-default tint.

Evidence Ui.kt:1049 tint-only expanded cue; :1048 static contentDescription.

Fix Add stateDescription = if(showTrash) "showing" else "hidden".

LowNo heading semantics on any screen titleSAccessibility (WCAG) 1.3.1, 2.4.10

SR users can't jump by heading; longer Settings/Sharing/Detail screens become a linear tab-hunt.

Evidence 0 heading() in app-desktop; titles are plain Text.

Fix Add Modifier.semantics { heading() } to the headlineMedium titles and titleLarge card headers.

LowFocus not returned on screen/dialog navigation — Back drops focus to the root windowLAccessibility (WCAG) 2.4.3

After Back from an item or leaving a sub-screen, focus lands on nothing, forcing a re-tab. Acknowledged A1b deferral.

Evidence Focus restoration absent on Ui.kt:505/1520/797/1038/1899 transitions; RecoverySetup confirm (395) no autoFocus.

Fix In A1b: capture a FocusRequester on the invoking element/list and restore on return; add autoFocus to the RecoverySetup confirm field.

LowUnlock's Argon2id wait drops the 'Unlock' label for a bare spinner — missing 'Unsealing…' voiceSMicrocopy/voice + interaction affordance

During the multi-second key derivation a sighted user sees an unlabeled spinner where the action word was; breaks the shared 'Unsealing…' voice. SR unaffected.

Evidence Ui.kt:2372-2377 bare spinner while busy; used for Unlock (422). Web/ext 'Unsealing…' at Welcome.tsx:153/370, popup.ts:690.

Fix Keep the word during busy: render 'Unsealing…' (spinner optional) in Primary on the auth path.

LowVault list uses a non-lazy Column(verticalScroll).forEach — search re-filters and re-composes all rows — MERGEDMInteraction performance / states

The primary browse surface composes every filtered row (offscreen included) and re-runs the whole filter+compose on each search keystroke — visible lag/jank only at larger vault sizes.

Evidence Ui.kt:531-534 non-lazy forEach; :433-445 filter per keystroke; 0 LazyColumn (grep); ImportBucket cap comment 714-729.

Fix Switch the vault list (and Trash/version-history enumerations) to LazyColumn keyed by itemId.

LowNull-but-not-busy list states render an empty gap (Trash, Version history)SState coverage

Between opening Trash/History and the first fetch tick, a blank area reads as 'broken/empty' rather than 'loading'.

Evidence Ui.kt:809 and :1480 render "" for the null-but-not-yet-busy state.

Fix Show 'Loading…' for the null state regardless of busy, or drive a Loading/Empty/Loaded sealed state.

LowEnroll consent checkbox and the next screen give contradictory recovery stakesSMicrocopy consistency

One screen says only the offline key can reset the master password; the next says the user's own phrase (and the admin) can — a trust-eroding contradiction at the ZK-stakes moment, and arguably now inaccurate given self-recovery shipped.

Evidence Ui.kt:322 vs :378.

Fix Align the checkbox to acknowledge both real recovery avenues (recovery phrase + household/admin key).

LowVault-row item names hard-clip (maxLines=1, default Clip) while the badge beside them ellipsizesSResponsive/platform-fit (long names)

A long name is cut mid-glyph rather than ellipsized, reading as a glitch and hiding disambiguating tails — inconsistent with the adjacent badge.

Evidence Ui.kt:764 maxLines=1 no overflow; :780 badge Ellipsis.

Fix Add overflow = TextOverflow.Ellipsis to Ui.kt:764.

LowShown-once recovery-phrase reveal has no shoulder-surf/screenshot cue and leans on an auto-lock that may be offSPassword-manager trust-UX (recovery-phrase ceremony)

The one-time phrase can sit visible on an unattended desktop indefinitely on a zero-auto-lock config, with no 'don't screenshot / cover your screen' guidance.

Evidence Ui.kt:357-401 reveal; comment 348-350 relies on auto-lock; DesktopState.kt:966 window<=0 disables it; default 0.

Fix Add an explicit shoulder-surf/screenshot caution line and enforce a minimum client-side auto-lock on this screen regardless of policy.

NitWordmark is monochrome — no two-tone gold 'and' on desktopSVisual design, brand & iconography

The gold-'and' lockup is web-only; desktop (like Android) is flat one color — a missed cheap brand cue, not an inconsistency.

Evidence Ui.kt:139 titleLarge, no color override; 0 buildAnnotatedString/SpanStyle in app-desktop.

Fix Optional: buildAnnotatedString with SpanStyle(primary) on 'and'. Low value given deliberate Android parity.

The invite email (every member’s first contact) and the web app shell — never in the prior accessibility pass.

HighInvite email is unbranded plaintext with no inviter identity — the first contact for every member is phishing-indistinguishable (merges V1 visual + U2 ux + T1 trust + U5 inviter-identity)MVisual / UX / Trust-UX (merged, 3 lenses same defect)

The single highest-stakes first-contact — an unsolicited message asking a household member to click a link and set up a password manager — arrives as an unstyled plaintext block from a mismatched no-reply@monahanhosting.com domain, with a link whose origin matches neither the brand nor the sender, no named inviter, and no anti-phishing anchor. A recipient has no legitimacy signal to distinguish it from a credential-harvest lure.

Evidence EmailSender.kt:84 msg.setText(body,"utf-8") is a single text/plain part; GraphEmailSender.kt:78 contentType "Text"; body byte-identical (EmailSender.kt:89-100 / GraphEmailSender.kt:95-106) — no wordmark, colour, rune, inoculation line, or inviter name. Subject hardcoded "You're invited to andvari". from = InternetAddress(from) no display name (EmailSender.kt:78); Graph sender is a raw no-reply UPN (GraphEmailSender.kt:41). Brand assets already exist in-app: tagline (Welcome.tsx:204), BrandSigil (Sigil.tsx:12-15), gold a-mark (styles.css:104).

Fix Cheapest high-value fix is COPY-ONLY, no HTML risk: (1) plumb the inviter display name through sendInvite/createInvite → open with "<Name> invited you to andvari, your household's vault"; (2) add a fixed inoculation line: "andvari will never email, call, or message you to ask for your master password or recovery phrase"; (3) add the tagline "the keeper of the hoard" as a signature. Optionally a multipart/alternative HTML part reusing existing tokens (--gold #d0a94a on --bg #14120e, inline BrandSigil geometry, gold-button anchor) keeping plaintext as the fallback part.

HighEmail body promises a "printed recovery sheet ... to finish setting up" that the DEFAULT emailed (waived) flow never producesMUX flows / microcopy correctness

The email is one static string sent for both postures, but the common emailed case is waived, where the member generates their OWN recovery phrase on-device and no admin-provided printed sheet exists. Telling every recipient they "also need the printed recovery sheet" is factually wrong for the default flow — they wait for / hunt a sheet the admin never produced, stalling the most important onboarding ceremony, and it contradicts the app's correct self-service message.

Evidence EmailSender.kt:97 & GraphEmailSender.kt:103 (identical): "You'll also need the printed recovery sheet, handed to you in person, to finish setting up." But App.kt:636-641 comment states the Admin UI defaults emailed invites to "waived"; enrollposture.test.ts:16 enrollPosture(undefined,false)→"waived"; account.ts:96 "Pass neither for a waived invite → no escrow blob"; account.ts:97 the member-generated self-service recovery piece is MANDATORY.

Fix Branch body() on the invite's escrowPolicy (already on the invite row, passed at App.kt:642): waived → drop the sheet line and prime the real stakes ("you'll create a recovery phrase during setup — it's the ONLY way back into your account, keep it somewhere safe"); required → keep the printed-sheet line.

MediumNo favicon anywhere, and the catch-all mis-serves index.html as /favicon.ico (merges V2 + T2 + favicon parts of A2/U4)SVisual / Trust recognizability / platform-fit

A password manager users keep pinned/tab-hunt for shows the browser-default globe — reads as unfinished for a security tool and weakens spoof-distinguishing recognition. The /favicon.ico-returns-HTML behaviour is a benign correctness oddity (the browser discards the wrong content-type, still no icon).

Evidence web/index.html:1-13 and web/dist/index.html have no <link rel=icon>; web/public holds only .well-known/assetlinks.json (no icon asset). App.kt:747-762 SPA catch-all serves File(root, safe.ifEmpty{"index.html"}), falling back to index.html for any non-file path, via respondFileContent → ContentType.defaultForFile — so GET /favicon.ico returns index.html as 200 text/html. BrandSigil geometry exists (Sigil.tsx:12-15); CSP img-src 'self' data: (App.kt:759) makes a data-URI SVG icon CSP-clean.

Fix Add <link rel="icon" href="data:image/svg+xml,..."> built from the BrandSigil paths (gold #d0a94a stroke on #14120e) plus an apple-touch-icon PNG in web/public. Optionally return 404 for /favicon.ico in the catch-all instead of index.html.

Medium"Open this link on the same network as the app" is opaque jargon and offers no fallback when the link won't loadSUX flows / microcopy

A new member doesn't know which network the app lives on or how to get onto it; when the link fails there is no troubleshooting line and no 'ask the person who invited you' — a hard dead-end at step one with the 1-hour clock running.

Evidence EmailSender.kt:92 / GraphEmailSender.kt:98 is the ONLY navigational instruction. Register is refused on the public origin (App.kt:335 throw Forbidden("register_public_disabled")), so the link must be opened against a private/LAN/Tailscale origin; the link expires within the hour (body line 96).

Fix Replace with concrete guidance + recovery path, e.g. 'Open this on the device your household set up for andvari (on your home Wi-Fi or Tailscale). If the page doesn't load, message the person who invited you before the hour is up.' Ideally name the expected host.

LowNo theme-color meta — mobile browser chrome stays light around the dark-first charcoal app (merges V3 + theme parts of A2/U4/T4)SVisual / platform-fit

On mobile Chrome/Android the address/status bar tints to theme-color; with none set it stays default white/grey around the deep-charcoal card, a light frame that breaks the dark-first immersion.

Evidence web/index.html:6 sets only color-scheme; no <meta name=theme-color> in source or dist. App ground --bg #14120e dark / #f4efe4 light (styles.css:10,40).

Fix Add <meta name="theme-color" media="(prefers-color-scheme: dark)" content="#14120e"> and a light counterpart content="#f4efe4">.

LowNo PWA web app manifest — web client not installable, 'Add to Home Screen' yields default icon + URL name (merges V4 + manifest parts of A2/U4/T4)MVisual / platform-fit (PWA installability)

An iOS/desktop/web-first user who 'installs' the site gets the browser default icon and the URL string as the label — no andvari name, rune, or charcoal splash — and forgoes standalone display.

Evidence No *.webmanifest / manifest.json for the web app (find over web/ returns only the desktop /downloads/manifest.json server route, App.kt:307); index.html has no <link rel=manifest> or apple-touch-icon. web/public/.well-known/assetlinks.json ships a native Android app (com.silencelen.andvari) but that does not make the WEB app installable.

Fix Add manifest.webmanifest (name andvari, theme/background #14120e, display standalone, maskable 192/512 icons from the rune) + <link rel=manifest>; reuse the favicon rune asset.

LowNo Open Graph / social meta — an enroll link pasted into chat unfurls as a bare URL (merges V5 + T5)SVisual / trust legibility

When an admin forwards the enroll link in iMessage/Signal/Slack instead of email, the unfurl shows no title card, wordmark, or reassuring description — the same generic-link deficit at first contact.

Evidence web/index.html has <title> only — no og:*/twitter:* tags. Enroll flow is explicitly person-to-person / chat-shareable (EnrollLink.kt:11 'one-scan onboarding', :35 'chat apps hard-wrap b64'); email is off by default (App.kt:118 email = null).

Fix Add static og:title "andvari", og:description (the tagline), og:type, and a self-contained og:image (rune on charcoal) + twitter equivalents to index.html.

LowApp shell has no <noscript> fallback — blank, AT-invisible page on JS-off / bundle-fail / CSP-blockSAccessibility / robustness

If the JS bundle fails, is disabled, or is CSP-blocked, the document delivers only <title>andvari</title> — a screen-reader user lands on an empty document with no heading/landmark/message, on the first page every emailed enrollee hits.

Evidence web/index.html:9-12 body is <div id=root> + module script; dist/index.html identical (script hoisted to head), no <noscript> (grep NONE). UI is fully client-rendered (main.tsx:12 createRoot). The a11y A1 pass was explicitly 'Zero server changes' (accessibility-a1.md:8) so the server HTML shell was never in its finding set. Strict CSP at App.kt:759.

Fix Add a branded <noscript> sentence inside <body> ('andvari needs JavaScript to unseal your hoard — enable it, then reload'); optionally a minimal static <main> skeleton so the document is never empty (edit web/index.html; Vite copies it verbatim).

LowThe ~223-char opaque base64 enroll link in a plaintext email risks broken/partial linkificationMPlatform-fit (email/MUA rendering) / trust legibilityplausible

Plaintext MUAs that hard-wrap at ~72-78 cols and auto-linkify to the first whitespace could produce a clickable link truncated at the wrap; a single click then loads a partial URL, the fragment parses to null (silent empty prefill), and enrollment appears broken with no error.

Evidence Link shape <origin>/enroll#a1.<b64url> (EnrollLink.kt:27, composed at App.kt:655 via EnrollLink.compose). Token is 32 random bytes (ServerCrypto newToken = Bytes.toB64(crypto.randomBytes(32))) → 43 b64 chars; the full fragment b64-encodes {v,o,t,e}. It sits raw on one plaintext line (EmailSender.kt:94 / GraphEmailSender.kt:100). EnrollLink.parse strips embedded whitespace (EnrollLink.kt:117) so a fully copy-pasted link recovers.

Fix Send a multipart/alternative HTML part whose <a href> holds the full URL intact with friendly link text (keeping plaintext as fallback); at minimum add a plaintext hint 'if the link looks cut off, copy the whole thing into your browser.' Same HTML-email work as the branding finding.

LowExtension overlay --anv-ink-faint still holds the pre-A1 sub-AA values (#6f6752/#9a8f74), and no contrast test covers content-ui.tsSAccessibility (WCAG)

The content-script overlay injected into third-party login pages carries its own --anv-* token set left on the pre-accessibility faint values while popup.css/styles.css were fixed. Latent today (the token is unconsumed) but an unguarded trap: the next overlay label reaching for the parallel faint token lands below 4.5:1 with no test to catch it.

Evidence content-ui.ts:30 --anv-ink-faint #6f6752 (dark) and :51 #9a8f74 (light) — the exact values A1 raised OFF (accessibility-a1.md:128-129: #6f6752→#8d8370, #9a8f74→#786c50; web styles.css:19/48 fixed). Both contrast tests read only styles.css (web contrast.test.ts:16) / popup.css (extension contrast.test.ts:31), never content-ui.ts. grep var(--anv-ink-faint) → zero consumers (dead token). The overlay's actually-used dim text uses --anv-ink-dim #a79c85 (content-ui.ts:29), which passes.

Fix Update content-ui.ts:30/:51 to #8d8370/#786c50 (or delete the dead token), then extend the extension contrast test to also parse the --anv-* map against --anv-bg/--anv-bg-input.

LowContrast unit tests cover only a narrow token set — button-ink-on-gold, danger/ok, tags, disabled, and focus-ring contrast are unverifiedMAccessibility / test-coverage

The computed-ratio safety net protects only the two tokens A1 changed; a future palette tweak to the button ink, status tones, or focus ring regresses silently. Scope note — on this server-HTML surface the tests protect nothing, but the shell is the entry point to those unguarded screens.

Evidence web contrast.test.ts:57-66 loops only ["--ink-faint","--link"] × {--bg,--bg-raised}. No pair for --btn-ink↔--gold, --danger/--ok tone text, .tag, disabled, or the focus-ring↔surface 3:1 (WCAG 1.4.11). A1 deferred prefers-reduced-motion/focus-visible/skip-links (accessibility-a1.md:499).

Fix Broaden both contrast tests to enumerate the full text/UI token pair set (button-ink↔gold, danger/ok↔bg & bg-raised, placeholder↔bg-input, focus-ring↔surface at the 3:1 UI threshold). Pure test-file change.

NitInvite email sets no branded From display-name — appears in the inbox list as a raw mailbox addressSVisual / trust (inbox-list view)

The inbox row (sender name + subject) is seen before the body is opened; a raw no-reply@monahanhosting.com in the From column reads as a random mailbox, not andvari.

Evidence EmailSender.kt:78 InternetAddress(from) with no personal/display name; GraphEmailSender payload (lines 74-86) has no from.emailAddress.name and sends via /users/{sender}/sendMail with the raw UPN. Subject is branded fine.

Fix InternetAddress(from, "andvari") for SMTP; add "from": { emailAddress: { name: "andvari", address: sender } } to the Graph message payload.

Nit"it can't be undone once used" is ambiguous phrasing for a single-use linkSUX flows / microcopy

'Undone' implies a reversible action being rolled back; the intended meaning is 'stops working after one use.' A non-technical reader can misread it as a warning that clicking does something irreversible/scary.

Evidence EmailSender.kt:96 / GraphEmailSender.kt:102: "This link is a one-time key — it expires within the hour, and it can't be undone once used."

Fix "This link works only once, and only for the next hour."

NitPlaintext email is the accessible choice — record a11y guardrails BEFORE any brand/phishing HTML redesignSAccessibility (advisory constraint)

From the a11y lens the current plaintext is a strength (clean SR read, inherits reader font/contrast, no image-only text). The other lenses will push to make it branded HTML; this attaches the a11y constraints to that redesign in advance so it doesn't regress.

Evidence EmailSender.kt:84 / GraphEmailSender.kt:78 plaintext body. Gold #d0a94a on charcoal #14120e ≈8.4:1 (consistent with styles.css:25 noting dark --gold at 7.7:1 on the lighter --bg-raised); the a11y risk is the faint/dim ink family (#6f6752/#9a8f74), not the gold.

Fix If it becomes HTML: keep the plaintext as the text/plain alternative; never render the wordmark/tagline/link as an image without alt; give the enroll link descriptive anchor text ('Set up your andvari account') not the raw token URL; keep heading/body at gold-on-charcoal and avoid the faint ink tokens.

Screenshot gallery23

The live web app, rendered headless with synthetic fixtures — no real secrets. Native clients have no headless render path and were audited from source.

Multi-agent UI audit · 20 finder agents × 4 lenses, cross-platform consistency stage, per-surface adversarial verification (9 claims rejected), synthesis. Native = code-only; web = fixture-rendered. Security/crypto correctness out of scope.

Companion markdown: docs/design/2026-07-12-frontend-ui-audit.md
